title: feat(m6): triple shadow signal — wire editor + reviewer roles to recordShadowDecision
body:
## Summary

Extends today's architect-only `recordShadowDecision` wiring (PR #362) to all three roles (architect, editor, reviewer). Each task now writes 3 shadow rows instead of 1 — tripling the M6 live-bandit signal volume and halving time-to-KPI for the 25% cost-per-task gate.

Zero behaviour change on live routing — `routing.architect.model` still runs the task. Editor + reviewer shadow rows accumulate alongside for per-role learning-curve comparison.

## In scope

- **Schema migration** (`src/queue/store.ts`): `role TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'architect'` column on `routing_shadow_log` + idempotent ALTER for existing DBs + `idx_routing_shadow_role` index.
- **Public API change** (`src/agents/bandit/shadow.ts`): `ShadowDecisionInput` / `ShadowDecisionRecord` gain a required `role: 'architect' | 'editor' | 'reviewer'` field. INSERT and `readShadowDecisions` SELECT updated. Breaking change to the only external caller (factory.ts), which is updated in the same PR.
- **Factory loop** (`src/pipeline/factory.ts`): `for (const role of ROLES)` over the three roles. Each role's call is wrapped in its **own** try/catch so a failure on (e.g.) editor never skips reviewer. `setRoutingDecision` moved above the loop (still under the `opts.taskStore` guard).
- **Tests**: every existing `ShadowDecisionInput` construction now passes `role: 'architect'` (preserves baseline behaviour). New tests: editor row writes role=editor, `readShadowDecisions` returns role on each row, schema migration idempotency on second `TaskStore` construction, `PRAGMA table_info` shape check on `routing_shadow_log.role`, source-grep for the ROLES loop, source-grep for the per-role try/catch. The integration test in `repo-resolver.test.ts` now expects 3 shadow rows and asserts each role row's `actualModel` matches the corresponding `routing[role].model`.

## Deferred

- **Live bandit override** — gated on a 25% cost-per-task win in `routing_shadow_log`. Shadow rows accumulate now; the live cutover is the M6 closure step, not this PR.
- Bandit-driven model selection for editor / reviewer paths in the live routing — same gate.

## Migration safety

- Forward-only, backward-compatible: `DEFAULT 'architect'` backfills existing rows to the correct value (all pre-this-PR rows came from architect-only wiring).
- ALTER is wrapped in a try/catch that swallows the SQLite `duplicate column` error so re-running on a fresh DB (where the CREATE TABLE already included `role`) is a no-op. `migration is idempotent` test exercises this.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` clean
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/queue src/agents/bandit src/pipeline` — 220 passing
- [x] `pnpm test` full suite — 885 vitest + 473 node tests, 0 failures
- [x] Source-grep tests for ROLES loop + per-role try/catch
- [x] Schema migration idempotency across two `TaskStore` constructions
- [x] Integration test asserts `actualModel` per role matches `routing[role].model`

title: fix(m4.6): mode-override no longer downshifts canonical-Opus architect assignment
body:
## Summary

Closes the M4.6 follow-up tracked in [ADR-0004 §Known limitations item 1](../blob/main/docs/adr/0004-canonical-routing-alignment.md#known-limitations--tracked-follow-ups). The structural protection — the `categoryOverrideTriggered` flag plus the suppression branch in the mode-override block — landed in PR #301 (2026-06-03). This PR closes the two gaps that remained on that work:

- **Observability.** The suppression branch silently skipped the mode demotion, so anyone debugging routing had no way to see which precedence rule had won. A warn-level log at the suppression site now cites canonical §3.2 override #1 and the mode that was suppressed.
- **Exhaustive contract coverage.** A 4×4 `(mode, category)` matrix in `src/classifier/__tests__/classifier.test.ts` pins the contract end-to-end: every demoting mode (`tdd`, `ulw`, `ralph`, `deslop`) × every canonical category (`security`, `auth`, `payments`, `migration`) asserts both that architect=Opus is preserved AND that the suppression warn is emitted. Two negative baselines confirm the warn does not fire when the category override hasn't triggered, or when no mode is set.

ADR-0004 §Known limitations item 1 amended to record this follow-up; M4.7 and M4.8 remain marked as resolved per their original closure (no change to those entries).

## Scope

- **IN scope (M4.6 only):** `src/classifier/index.ts` warn log + tests + ADR amendment.
- **DEFERRED:**
  - M4.7 — explicit `category:*` / `severity:*` label parsing (already resolved in PR #304; not touched here).
  - M4.8 — reviewer derivation after mode overrides (already resolved in PR #301; not touched here).

## References

- Canonical: `~/.claude/skills/CANONICAL-PATTERN.md` §3.2 override #1 — "Category ∈ {security, auth, payments, migration} → Opus regardless of severity."
- ADR: `docs/adr/0004-canonical-routing-alignment.md` §Known limitations item 1.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` clean.
- [x] `pnpm test` — 490 node:test + 881 vitest tests all pass (18 new M4.6 cases included).
- [x] `node --import tsx --test 'src/classifier/**/*.test.ts'` — all classifier suites green, including the 16-case matrix.
- [x] Baseline cases verified: mode without HIGH_KEYWORDS still demotes to Sonnet (no warn); HIGH_KEYWORD without mode still routes to Opus (no warn).

title: feat(m6): persist routing_decision + shadow-log Thompson pick
body:
## Summary

M6-T3 — wires the shadow-eval substrate into live routing. Every dispatched task now persists its `RoutingDecision` to `tasks.routing_decision` and records a Thompson sample to `routing_shadow_log`. The bandit can finally learn from real `(arm, reward)` observations as `pr_decisions` accrues. **Zero behaviour change to live routing** — `routing.architect.model` is still the model that runs.

## In scope (architect role only)

- **Schema**: idempotent `ALTER TABLE tasks ADD COLUMN routing_decision TEXT` (added to `SCHEMA` + post-construct ALTER backstop for pre-M6 DBs).
- **Write path**: `TaskStore.setRoutingDecision(taskId, decision)` + `TaskStore.getDb()` accessor. `QueuedTask` gains `routingDecision?: RoutingDecision | null`.
- **Observations reader**: `src/agents/bandit/observations.ts` — `buildShadowObservations(db, repo, role)`. SQL joins `pr_decisions.verdict` against `json_extract(tasks.routing_decision, '$.<role>.model')`. `merged → 1`, `rejected → 0`, `abandoned` filtered out. NULL routing_decision and malformed JSON tolerated (both yield no observations).
- **Known arms**: `src/agents/bandit/known-arms.ts` — `KNOWN_MODEL_IDS` replicates the alias list from `src/orchestrator/handlers/boot-config.ts:18-23`. Boot-config stays the canonical bootstrap-time check; we don't refactor it.
- **Wiring**: `src/pipeline/factory.ts` calls `setRoutingDecision` + `recordShadowDecision` immediately after `classifyTask(...)`. Wrapped in `try/catch` so shadow failures never break live routing (`recordShadowDecision` is already internally fail-open; `buildShadowObservations` is the synchronous risk the outer catch covers).
- **Daemon**: `makeProductionFactory` takes new optional `taskStore: TaskStore` opt; daemon passes `store`.

## Deferred (intentional)

- **Editor / reviewer roles**: only `architect` is shadowed this PR. Editor and reviewer arms come in a follow-up — the observations reader already accepts the role enum so the bandit will pick them up with one-line callsite changes.
- **Live override**: the shadow pick is logged, never used. The 25% cost-per-task win gate (M6 closure) is unchanged.

## Migration safety

- Forward-only and backward-compatible. Pre-M6 rows stay `NULL`; the observations reader filters them out (`WHERE t.routing_decision IS NOT NULL`).
- The `ALTER` is idempotent against the SQLite-doesn't-support-`IF NOT EXISTS` rule via the `duplicate column name` regex swallow — same pattern as the existing `priority` and `attempts` columns.
- `routing_shadow_log` accumulates rows from this commit forward only. Historical analytics will treat the pre-PR window as empty, which is correct.

## Decisions worth flagging to T1 reviewer

- **`KNOWN_MODEL_IDS` was replicated, not imported**: importing `KNOWN_MODEL_SHORTHAND` from `boot-config.ts` would drag daemon-boot wiring into the routing call site (and the boot-config set mixes aliases + full IDs; the bandit needs full IDs only because `mapModel()` always emits them). Per the brief's "do NOT refactor boot-config" guard.
- **Observations reader builds the JSON path string inline, not as a bound parameter**: `json_extract`'s second arg can't be a SQL parameter in better-sqlite3. The role is whitelisted via a runtime enum check before string concatenation, so no injection surface.
- **`opts.taskStore` is optional**: keeps existing factory test fixtures and `repo-resolver` tests compiling without threading a sqlite handle through. Live daemon wiring passes it; tests that don't care about the shadow log can omit it.
- **`pr_decisions.verdict = 'abandoned'`**: contract from `shadow.ts` is "don't emit a third reward state". Abandoned PRs surface as zero observations, which is conservative (no implicit penalty against the model).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` clean.
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/queue src/agents/bandit src/pipeline` — 214 tests passing.
- [x] `pnpm test` (full suite) — 854 vitest + 467 node tests passing.
- [x] New tests:
  - `store.test.ts` — `setRoutingDecision` round-trip + `getDb` returns the handle with `routing_shadow_log` present (2 new).
  - `observations.test.ts` — merged/rejected/abandoned mapping, NULL filtering, per-role distinct arm sets, malformed JSON tolerance, repo filtering, unknown-role rejection (6 new).
  - `factory.test.ts` — source-level guard that the wiring stays in place + the try/catch wraps it (2 new).
  - `repo-resolver.test.ts` — integration covering (a) live persist + shadow_log INSERT and (b) no-store back-compat path (2 new).

title: fix(m5.2): recordProposalDecision first-write-wins guard · [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-50b49e86]
body:
## Problem

`recordProposalDecision` in `goal-proposals-store.ts` issued an unconditional `UPDATE … WHERE id = $1` — no guard on the current `decision` value. This meant:

- An authorised approver could click **Approve** then **Reject** (or **Approve** twice), flipping the row's `decision` and `decided_by` / `decided_at`.
- Each Approve click re-ran `enqueueApprovedProposal`, firing another `/ship` POST. The `proposal:<id>` idempotency key collapsed the duplicate task at the control-plane level, but a deliberate Approve → Reject sequence left an orphaned outcome: a spawned task in flight with the proposal row marked `rejected` and `resulting_task_id` cleared.

## Fix

### SQL guard (`goal-proposals-store.ts`)

```sql
UPDATE goal_proposals
   SET decision = $2,
       decided_by = $3,
       decided_at = $4,
       resulting_task_id = CASE WHEN $2 = 'approved' THEN NULL ELSE resulting_task_id END
 WHERE id = $1
   AND decision IS NULL      -- ← first-write-wins
 RETURNING id
```

When `rowCount = 0`, a follow-up `SELECT decision` distinguishes two sub-cases and surfaces them through the new `existing_decision?` field on `RecordDecisionResult`:
- **Row exists, already decided** → `{ updated: false, existing_decision: 'approved' | … }`
- **Row missing** → `{ updated: false }` (no `existing_decision` key)

### Handler branch (`interaction-create.ts`)

```
!result.updated
  existing_decision != null  →  "⚠ Proposal `<id>` already decided (<decision>)."
                                 skip enqueueApprovedProposal
  else                       →  existing "proposal pruned" reply (unchanged)
```

### Tests added

**`goal-proposals-store.test.ts`** (3 new cases):
- `first-write-wins: second decision on a decided row returns updated:false and existing_decision`
- `Approve does NOT reset resulting_task_id when row is already approved (rowCount 0)`
- `Reject on an already-Approved row returns updated:false + existing_decision:approved`

**`interaction-create.test.ts`** (1 new case):
- `proposal_approve on already-decided row replies "already decided" and does NOT call enqueueApprovedProposal`

## Verification

- `pnpm typecheck` — clean
- `pnpm vitest run src/orchestrator src/discord` — 93/93 passed

title: feat(m5.2): confidence-gated auto-approve for high-score proposals
body:
## Summary

Closes the **"Approve gate on every proposal"** autonomy bottleneck. The proposer pipeline now splits `enforceBudget`'s top survivors by `composite_score >= threshold` and routes each subset to its own sink:

- **auto path** (`>= threshold`) — `insertProposal` → `recordProposalDecision({decision: 'approved', decidedBy: 'auto-bandit-<threshold>'})` → `controlPlane.postCommand` (`sprint_goal`, idempotency `proposal:<id>`) → `setResultingTaskId`. Skips the Discord click entirely.
- **HITL path** (`< threshold`) — existing `postProposalsForApproval` (Approve / Reject / Defer buttons on `#ifleet-proposals`).

New env var `IFLEET_PROPOSALS_AUTO_APPROVE_THRESHOLD` (default `1.0` = HITL only, **no behavioural change** vs M5.2-T1). Sebastian's intended dial after the first week of picks is `0.85`; `0.0` is fully autonomous.

## Why the verifier is still load-bearing

Auto-approve removes the Discord click on **goal selection** — it does not remove the verifier gate on **PR correctness**. Every auto-enqueued sprint still flows through architect → reviewer → verifier exactly as today; a bad pick fails to merge rather than landing un-reviewed. The threshold is a confidence dial on _which_ goals to chase, not on _how_ to ship them.

The decided-by audit trail records `auto-bandit-<threshold>` so `goal_proposals` rows make auto vs human decisions distinguishable forever.

## Failure semantics

Per-candidate errors log + skip without blocking the rest of the batch:
- `insertProposal` throws → skip this id, continue.
- `recordProposalDecision` returns `updated=false` → skip enqueue (row vanished between insert + decision).
- `controlPlane.postCommand` throws OR `ack.accepted === false` → log + skip `setResultingTaskId`.
- `setResultingTaskId` failure → log + still count (task is queued; M5.2-T2 reverse linker reconciles via `extractProposalIdFromIdempotencyKey`).

## DI seams

`ControlPlaneClient` is threaded through new `RunProposerOptions.deps`. The cron entry (`scripts/proposer-run.ts`) constructs an `HmacControlPlaneClient` from `CONTROL_PLANE_URL` + `IFLEET_HMAC_SECRET`; when either is unset the orchestrator falls back to HITL-only with a warn (so smoke runs without the daemon don't crash).

## M6 hook

The threshold is a plain number today; M6 closure can feed the Thompson-sampling bandit's shadow data (`routing_shadow_log`, PR #354) into a per-source threshold without changing this seam.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm typecheck` clean
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/agents/proposer src/discord` — 148 tests, all green
- [x] Pre-push hook (`pnpm test`) — 837 tests, all green
- [ ] After merge: bump `IFLEET_PROPOSALS_AUTO_APPROVE_THRESHOLD=0.85` in `/etc/environment` on the VPS to enable the auto-approve path
- [ ] Watch a week of `goal_proposals` rows (`decided_by LIKE 'auto-bandit-%'`) and adjust threshold if false-positives spike

title: feat(m6): shadow-eval harness substrate (knocks down 1 of 2 self-deploy preconditions)
body:
## Summary

- Adds the shadow-eval harness substrate at `src/agents/shadow-eval/` — replays a stored eval-set row through mockable architect + verifier seams, compares against the row's recorded expected outcome, emits per-row PASS/FAIL with a structured reason.
- **Substrate only.** Does NOT wire into deploy gating yet. That's M6 closure. The point of landing it now is to have the harness exist so the eval-set-growth work that follows has somewhere to plug.
- Knocks down 1 of the 2 remaining ❌ entries in the self-deploy precondition list (ROADMAP.md line 32: shadow-eval harness exists). The other ❌ (eval-set ≥50 rows; currently 13) is a separate session.

## Schema

`EvalRow` is inferred from the live `.ifleet/eval/eval-set.jsonl` as of 2026-06-16 (matches `.ifleet/eval/README.md`):

```
id, issue_url, pr_url, repo, title, body,
classifier_label_actual, diff_url, diff_summary,
files_changed[], loc_added, loc_removed,
merged_at, reviewer_login, merge_decision, frozen_at
```

The three fields the harness actually compares (everything else is recorded for debugging): `classifier_label_actual`, `files_changed`, `merge_decision`.

## Files

- `src/agents/shadow-eval/types.ts` — `EvalRow`, `ExpectedOutcome`, `ActualOutcome`, `EvalResult`, `ShadowEvalSummary`.
- `src/agents/shadow-eval/replay.ts` — pure `compareEvalResult` (order-independent `filesChanged`, stable failure-precedence: classifierLabel → filesChanged → mergeDecision) + `replayRow(row, deps)`.
- `src/agents/shadow-eval/harness.ts` — `runShadowEval(deps)` reads JSONL, bounded concurrency (default 1, deterministic), preserves per-row order. `defaultReadEvalSet` tolerates blank lines and empty files.
- `src/agents/shadow-eval/cli.ts` — `node --import tsx src/agents/shadow-eval/cli.ts [--eval-set <path>] [--out <jsonl>]`. **Currently wires passthrough architect/verifier stubs** that echo the row back, so every row PASSes end-to-end — proves the pipeline works. M6 closure swaps these for real adapters against `src/agents/architect/` + `src/agents/verifier/`.
- `src/agents/shadow-eval/__tests__/replay.test.ts` — 10 cases (pure comparator + replayRow with mocked seams).
- `src/agents/shadow-eval/__tests__/harness.test.ts` — 5 cases (fixture eval-set, empty file, concurrency ordering, FAIL accounting).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/agents/shadow-eval` — 15 tests green.
- [x] Pre-push hook green (full 834-test vitest run).

title: feat(m6): Thompson sampling bandit + routing_shadow_log (shadow mode)
body:
## Summary

Closes **M6-T2** (see SPRINT.md row 5).

Lands the M6 economic-routing substrate: a per-model Thompson sampling bandit that records what it WOULD have picked next to every live routing decision, without overriding it. M6 closure will flip the override gate live, gated on a 25% cost-per-task win in shadow signal.

## What changed

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/agents/bandit/thompson.ts` | Pure module. `betaSample` (ratio-of-Gammas via Marsaglia–Tsang), `sampleArm` (argmax over Beta draws), `posteriorsFromObservations` (fold `{arm, reward}` stream into per-arm α/β with Beta(1, 1) default prior). RNG is injected everywhere — `Math.random` is only the default. |
| `src/agents/bandit/shadow.ts` | Integration seam. `recordShadowDecision(db, input)` computes the bandit pick and persists it; `readShadowDecisions` for the analytics path. Snapshots α/β/sample per arm as JSON so the dashboard can replay decisions later. |
| `src/queue/store.ts` | `TaskStore` constructor adds `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS routing_shadow_log` (FK to `tasks` via ON DELETE CASCADE) + the two access indexes. Same inline-migration pattern the M4 `pr_decisions` column uses. Schema: `id`, `task_id`, `repo`, `decided_at`, `actual_model`, `shadow_model`, `alpha_snapshot` (JSON), `beta_snapshot` (JSON), `sample_snapshot` (JSON). |

## Design notes

- **RNG is injected everywhere** — tests use a seeded LCG so "the bandit picks X given history Y" is deterministic.
- **Uniform prior Beta(1, 1)** so a never-tried model gets one chance to surface a wide sample. Caller can override.
- **`shadow_model` is computed but NEVER read by the routing decision.** That seam lands in M6 closure.
- **Snapshots are JSON blobs** so the analytics dashboard can replay a decision against a different sampler later (e.g. UCB) without re-running the live trace.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/agents/bandit` — 17 cases passing:
  - **thompson.test.ts** (11): α/β validation, [0,1] range over 50 random draws, mean concentration with a tight posterior, deterministic seed → same pick + samples, observations fold into the right α/β buckets, uniform-prior bootstrap, prior override.
  - **shadow.test.ts** (6): row persisted with snapshots + FK cascade, `actual_model` preserved verbatim, empty `knownArms` throws, newest-first read, FK delete cascade.
- [x] Full suite (818 vitest + node:test) green via pre-push hook.

## Out of scope

- Wiring `recordShadowDecision` into the actual routing call site. That's M6 closure — needs a call-site decision about WHEN to sample (per task, per repo, per model-class) and a backfill from `pr_decisions` on every sample.
- A dashboard widget. The snapshots are JSON-encoded so analytics work can land separately without touching this PR.
- Live override flip. Gated on the 25% cost-per-task signal in `routing_shadow_log`.

title: feat(m6): drift detector substrate (compare + scan)
body:
## Summary

Closes **M6-T1** (see SPRINT.md row 4).

Lands the M6 cross-repo coherence substrate: a pure comparator over the M3 knowledge graph that flags symbols whose signatures have drifted across peer repos OR which exist in some peer repos but not others.

This PR is **substrate only** — it computes a `DriftScanResult` and stops. Opening real drift PRs is M6 closure, gated on a ≥70% merge-rate KPI per the ROADMAP. Shadow signal first, live PRs later.

## What changed

All new files under `src/agents/drift-detector/`:

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `types.ts` | `SymbolObservation`, `DriftCandidate`, `DriftScanResult`. `orphan_reference` reserved in the `DriftKind` union for future cross_repo_links work; not emitted yet. |
| `compare.ts` | Pure comparator. Groups observations by `(kind, name)`. Emits `signature_skew` (≥2 distinct non-null signatures) and `rename_or_deletion` (present in some peerRepos, absent in others). NULL signatures are treated as "unknown", never as drift. Stable sort throughout — identical inputs → byte-identical outputs (the planner can diff runs). |
| `scan.ts` | DB entry. Reads `code_nodes` via `getKgPool()`, feeds the pure comparator, returns the result. Fail-open: KG unreachable or bad query → warn + empty result. Default kind filter is `{function, class, type, interface}` because those are the surfaces drift PRs are cheapest to write against. |

## Design notes

- **Null signatures are not drift.** Indexer couldn't extract a signature → unknown, not disagreement.
- **Majority wins.** `groups[0]` is always the most-populous signature; `outlierRepos` is the rest.
- **`peerRepos` is the universe.** Pass the active repo set from `config/channels.json`. Empty universe disables the rename/deletion test (signature_skew still fires).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/agents/drift-detector` — 13 cases passing:
  - **compare.test.ts** (10): signature_skew single + majority + null + identical; rename_or_deletion present + absent + universe-empty + all-present; determinism (input-order independence + kind isolation + minObservations).
  - **scan.test.ts** (3): <2-repos short-circuits; pool-mocked happy path surfaces both drift kinds from one query; pool-throws → fail-open with warn.
- [x] `pnpm test` green via pre-push hook.

## Out of scope

- DB migration. `code_nodes` already exists from M3.
- A planner that turns `DriftCandidate[]` into drift PRs — that's M6 closure.
- Cron wiring. Once a planner exists, M6 closure adds a PM2 entry similar to `ifleet-proposer`.
- `orphan_reference` emission (cross_repo_links integration) — `DriftKind` already lists it for forward-compat.

title: feat(m5.2): write PR outcome back onto goal_proposals row
body:
## Summary

Closes **M5.2-T2** (see SPRINT.md row 2).

M5.2-T1 wired Approve → /ship so an approved proposal spawns a task with idempotency key `proposal:<id>`. This PR closes the back-edge: when that task's PR merges or closes-unmerged, the outcome is pushed back onto the originating `goal_proposals` row so the Voyager iterative-prompting loop on the next nightly run has training data — "we tried this; reviewer merged / closed without merging."

## What changed

- `setResultingPrOutcome(proposalId, prUrl, outcome)` on `goal-proposals-store.ts`. `outcome` is one of `merged | rejected | closed_unmerged` (the canonical CHECK values from the M5 migration).
- `extractProposalIdFromIdempotencyKey(key)` — pure helper that returns the proposal id from a `proposal:<id>` key or null. Strict regex so Discord (`discord:…:…`) and GitHub (`github:…`) keys don't get misclassified.
- `recordPrOutcomeOnProposal` helper in `sprint-bridge.ts`. On `sprint.completed` with PR open → `merged`. On `sprint.failed` or `sprint.cancelled` with PR open → `closed_unmerged`. Fire-and-forget — failures log; the M4 pr_decisions row is recorded regardless.

## Outcome value mapping

| Sprint event | PR state | proposal outcome | rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sprint.completed` | merged | `merged` | The architect-side `pr_decisions` row also gets `verdict='merged'`. |
| `sprint.failed` w/ PR open | closed by us | `closed_unmerged` | Verifier failed; we closed our own PR. The reviewer never saw it. |
| `sprint.cancelled` w/ PR open | closed by us | `closed_unmerged` | Operator /cancel. Same reasoning. |
| _any_ | reviewer-rejected | (not emitted) | We don't have a reviewer-rejection signal at sprint-close. M6 can backfill from `pr_decisions` later. |

The `rejected` outcome value exists in the schema for completeness but is not emitted by this PR. Conflating "verifier closed before reviewer saw it" with "reviewer rejected the merged proposal" would poison the Voyager training data.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [x] `node --import tsx --test src/orchestrator/__tests__/goal-proposals-store.test.ts` — 10 new node:test cases covering the helper across happy + null + malformed + UUID inputs, and both store writes across row-match + row-missing + closed_unmerged param shape.
- [x] `pnpm test` green via pre-push hook.
- [ ] Live verification gated on M5.2-T3 deploy (VPS migration + #ifleet-proposals + PROPOSER_ENABLED=1).

## Out of scope

- M5.2-T3 deploy gate — needs Sebastian on VPS.
- M6 backfill of `rejected` outcomes from `pr_decisions.verdict='rejected'` scan.
- Voyager prompt that actually reads `getPastProposalsByRepo(...).resultingPrOutcome` — already wired in T3's context-loader; this PR makes it non-null for proposal-spawned tasks.

title: feat(m5.2): wire Discord Approve button → /ship enqueue
body:
## Summary

Closes **M5.2-T1** (see SPRINT.md row 1).

M5 substrate (#326) shipped the proposer pipeline + the Discord HITL gate, but the Approve button stopped at writing a row to `goal_proposals`. Sebastian had to manually re-fire `/ship <title>` to turn an approved proposal into a sprint goal. This PR closes the loop.

## What changed

- `getProposalForShip` + `setResultingTaskId` on `goal-proposals-store.ts` — one focused SELECT for the fields the enqueue path needs, one focused UPDATE for the task id the control plane returns.
- `enqueueApprovedProposal` helper in `interaction-create.ts`. On a Discord Approve click: record the decision (unchanged), fetch the proposal title + repo, POST a `sprint_goal` ControlCommand (idempotency key `proposal:<id>`), then write the resulting task id back onto the proposal row.
- Reply message now includes the task id: \`✔ Approved proposal \`p-99\` — enqueued as \`task-7\`.\`

## Failure semantics

| Failure | Behavior |
|---|---|
| DB load fails (getProposalForShip) | Warn in editReply; decision row stays; operator re-fires manually via `/ship`. |
| Control plane refusal (`accepted: false`) | Warn; decision row stays; no `resulting_task_id` written. |
| Control plane throws | Same as refusal. |
| Proposal pruned between Approve and enqueue | Warn "proposal row was missing"; no `/ship` POST. |
| Reject / Defer | Unchanged — no `/ship` POST. |

The intent is that an Approve always commits the decision row even if the downstream enqueue fails — losing the decision because the control plane was momentarily down is worse than a missing task link the operator can re-create with `/ship`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean.
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/discord src/orchestrator` — 92 cases passing, including 5 new for the M5.2 path: accept (asserts sprint_goal shape + setResultingTaskId(id, task) call + reply text), control-plane refusal, control-plane throw, Reject doesn't POST, proposal pruned.
- [ ] Live smoke test in #ifleet-proposals (gated on M5.2-T3 deploy — see SPRINT.md).

## Out of scope

- M5.2-T2 (PR outcome tracking) — next PR.
- M5.2-T3 deploy gate (VPS migration + env var set + pm2 restart) — needs Sebastian on VPS.
- Wiring the architect's prompt to read `getProposalForShip(...).rationale` as planning context — separate concern.

title: fix(observability): parent-trace passthrough for ADR-0001 sprint-trace compliance
body:
## Summary

- **AUDIT-IFleet-32646d9e (IMPORTANT):** `startTrace()` was creating one top-level Langfuse trace per Claude subprocess, producing N independent traces per sprint. ADR-0001:36-42 requires one shared trace per sprint with each subprocess as a generation/span under it. Fixed by checking `LANGFUSE_PARENT_TRACE_ID` env var: if set, attaches as a generation under the existing sprint trace; if absent, creates the sprint-level trace and exposes its ID via `LangfuseTrace.traceId` for sibling propagation.
- **AUDIT-IFleet-d641455e (COSMETIC):** The module docstring in `langfuse.ts` said "one trace per Claude subprocess invocation", contradicting ADR-0001:36-42. Updated to state the correct invariant with the correct ADR line citation.

Fixed by stripping `GIT_DIR`/`GIT_WORK_TREE` from `process.env` in `beforeEach`/`afterEach` and from the raw `execFileAsync` call that wasn't going through the `git()` helper.

title: fix(queue): atomic SqliteNonceLedger TOCTOU [AUDIT-IFleet-cf106efc]
body:
## Summary

Closes AUDIT-IFleet-cf106efc.

PR #319 narrowed but did not close the `SqliteNonceLedger.registerOrReject` TOCTOU race. The previous code ran prune → INSERT OR IGNORE as two unwrapped statements on a single better-sqlite3 handle, so two control-plane processes sharing the SQLite file could still race in the prune→insert window.

This PR wraps prune + INSERT OR IGNORE in a single `db.transaction().immediate(...)` so better-sqlite3 emits `BEGIN IMMEDIATE`. The IMMEDIATE write lock serialises concurrent writers and `result.changes === 1` IS the atomic fresh-or-replay decision. `SECURITY.md` updated to match (was: PR #319 INSERT-OR-IGNORE source of truth; now: prune+insert wrapped in BEGIN IMMEDIATE).

[audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-cf106efc]

## Test plan
- [x] New regression test in `src/queue/__tests__/store.test.ts`: two ledgers on the same SQLite file race on the same nonce → exactly one returns true
- [x] Companion test: prune still happens inside the same transaction as the insert (table doesn't grow beyond TTL window)
- [x] typecheck clean
- [x] existing `control-plane.test.ts` race regression still green (AUDIT-IFleet-63037351)

title: fix(quota): enforce single-seat Max-plan concurrency in code [AUDIT-IFleet-a394a4f1]
body:
## Summary

Closes AUDIT-IFleet-a394a4f1 (CRITICAL).

The Max-plan budget skip in `SprintManager.checkBudget` (src/orchestrator/sprint.ts:444-487) is correct — the Claude CLI's per-call USD field is not real spend on a flat-fee Max subscription, so enforcing BUDGET_USD would just fire spuriously. But that meant the only thing standing between three parallel Opus sessions and a burned overnight quota was `maxConcurrent: 3` in config/workers.json — a config-only constraint that drifts.

This PR:
- `validateMaxPlanConcurrency(workers)` in `src/orchestrator/workers.ts` — throws if any worker with `tier` starting `max-` has `maxConcurrent > 1`.
- Wired into BOTH load paths:
  - `WorkerRegistry.loadFromDisk` (watcher) — catches the throw, boots with zero workers, orchestrator refuses to dispatch.
  - `loadInitialWorkers` (bootstrap, daemon.ts) — throws uncaught, crashing the daemon. **Validation runs OUTSIDE the read-file try/catch** so a violation can't be swallowed and silently turned into env defaults (codex caught this on first iteration).
- `config/workers.json` — claude-max-1 / claude-max-2 maxConcurrent dropped from 3 → 1, notes flag the policy is now enforced in code.

## Caveats

Minimum-viable fix. The audit also requested a ccusage-aware nightly governor (token aggregation per authProfile per 5h window, optional `ccusage blocks --active --json` reads to seed reset times, automatic `/stop` on threshold). That's a real subsystem and is deferred. The single-seat cap here is the load-bearing part — without it, no token-aware governor would have a meaningful budget to police.

## Reviews

- Codex MCP review: PASS — *"v2 moves bootstrap validation outside the fallback catch, keeps watcher validation covered, and an invalid reload leaves zero available workers so dispatch cannot drain work"*

title: fix(security): narrow worker perms + deny destructive cmds [AUDIT-IFleet-dc95add8]
body:
## Summary

Closes AUDIT-IFleet-dc95add8 (CRITICAL).

Each Claude worker spawns with `--permission-mode acceptEdits` and reads a per-worktree `.claude/settings.json`. The previous allow list had `Bash(git *)` and `Bash(rm *)` — broad enough to permit `git push --force`, `git reset --hard`, `rm -rf`. Prompt-only rules at `src/pipeline/prompts.ts:68` cannot stop a hallucinating model from running them.

This PR:
- Narrows the allow list to read-only git aliases (`status`, `diff`, `log`, `show`, `rev-parse`, `branch --show-current`, `branch --list *`).
- Removes `Bash(find *)` (allows `find . -delete` / `find -exec rm`).
- Adds an explicit deny list for destructive forms, in BOTH bare and with-args shapes (Claude permission patterns are prefix/glob; `Bash(git push *)` does not cover bare `git push`).
- Extracts the object as `WORKER_CLAUDE_PERMISSIONS` for testability.

## Caveats

Defense-in-depth, NOT a sandbox. `Bash(node *)`/`npm`/`npx`/`pnpm`/`vitest` still let a worker execute arbitrary code (those are needed for verify steps). A real sandbox would need a syscall filter or container boundary. This PR removes the *trivial* destructive escape hatches; the harder ones are a follow-up.

## Reviews

- Claude cross-provider review: PASS (3rd iteration; first two FAILed on `Bash(find *)` and bare-form denies)
- Codex MCP review: PASS — *"removes the prior broad git/rm/find allows and now denies the high-risk git/rm/find forms in both bare and with-args shapes, so it materially improves the worker permission posture as intended"*

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run typecheck` — clean
- [x] `npx vitest run src/pipeline/__tests__/` — 182 tests pass
- [x] New regression test pins `Bash(git *)`, `Bash(rm *)`, `Bash(git branch *)`, `Bash(find *)` out of allow and into deny

title: AUDIT-IFleet-25566c6a/43254bcf: fix git-bare corruption + husky pre-push test isolation, per-lane worktree mandate
body:
## Summary

Closes 6 audit findings forming one causal chain (husky test contamination → bare=true corruption → HUSKY=0 bypass → no worktree isolation → cross-branch leakage).

Goal-tags:
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-25566c6a] git-bare config corruption
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-43254bcf] husky pre-push test contamination (root cause)
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-552a3c15] worktree isolation mandate
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-b294d83e] T5 HUSKY=0 bypass (retroactively authorized)
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-c069addc] HUSKY=0 hold-rule exception recorded
- [audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-f04b7806] T4 cross-lane test-edit documented

## Approach

Root cause: `src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-pr-decision.test.ts` inherited `GIT_DIR` / `GIT_WORK_TREE` / etc. from the husky `pre-push` hook's env. The tmpdir's `git init` wrote into the parent `.git/config` (flipping `core.bare=true`) and `git commit` landed `test@example.com` commits on the active worker branch — the exact failure mode documented in `docs/bug_prepush_test_contamination.md` and re-observed by T5 in PR #326 of the upstream session.

Fix in this PR:

1. **Test isolation.** Strip every inherited `GIT_*` env var before spawning git in `daemon-pr-decision.test.ts`. Pin `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` / `GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM` to scratch paths so the test never reads `~/.gitconfig` or `/etc/gitconfig`. After `git init`, assert (realpath-compared) that `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` from the tmpdir lands on the tmpdir — if env scrub regresses, the guard fails loudly inside the test, not silently in a future push.
2. **Regression guard.** Add `before` / `after` node:test hooks that snapshot the parent IFleet repo's `git log --oneline -5`, `core.bare`, and `HEAD` before the test file runs and assert no mutation after. Any future regression of the env scrub fails this file's after-hook, not a developer's push.
3. **Session-doc reconciliation (local-only — `splits/` is gitignored).** `MASTER.md` of the upstream `20260604-0910-m5-proposer-substrate` session gets a new `## Hold-rule exceptions` section recording T5's HUSKY=0 push of PR #326 as authorized retroactively by this PR; a per-lane worktree-add mandate is added under `## Lane assignments`; T4's cross-lane test-file edit is documented under `## Gate semantics`. `T5-done.md` gets a preamble pointing to the exception. `T1-done.md`'s CRITICAL pre-push entry is replaced with RESOLVED + back-reference.
4. **Orphan branch cleanup.** Local `feat/test` branch (artifact of prior contamination) deleted.

title: feat(m4): reviewer preference cards + get_reviewer_prefs tool
body:
## Summary

Closes M4-T3, M4-T4, and the M4-T6 KPI evidence path. One concern: the reviewer-preference pipeline + the architect tool that reads it.

- **`src/learning/reviewer-prefs/`** — reads `pr_decisions` over a rolling 30d window, groups by reviewer, writes top-N `<handle>.json` cards under `.ifleet/prefs/`. Pure summarisation (`summariseReviewerDecisions`) is split from I/O (`writeReviewerCard` / `buildReviewerCards`) so the projection is unit-testable on in-memory fixtures.
- **`scripts/build-reviewer-cards.ts`** — CLI entry for nightly regen. `pnpm reviewer-cards:build` (added to package.json). Idempotent: same DB → identical JSON bytes modulo `reviewed_at`.
- **`src/agents/architect/tools/get_reviewer_prefs.ts`** — `getReviewerPrefs(handle)` returns the `ReviewerCard` or `null` (not throws) when missing.
- **`src/agents/architect/tools/index.ts`** — first version of an architect tool registry. Today it just aggregates `query_code_graph` + `get_reviewer_prefs` and exposes an `ARCHITECT_TOOLS` array. Picked because the existing `src/agents/architect/tools/` had no central registry; this aligns with T2.md guidance to "find the registry" or, failing that, expose a clean one.
- **`vitest.config.ts`** — picks up the new `src/learning/` test tree.

## M4-T6 KPI evidence

Query (run against the canonical `state/tasks.db` via better-sqlite3):

```sql
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
  SUM(CASE WHEN fingerprint IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS fp_rows
FROM pr_decisions;
```

**Result against the deployed VPS DB at `root@187.124.77.142:/opt/ifleet/state/tasks.db`:**

```
total_rows = 10
fp_rows    = 0        (column missing — see Surprises)
ratio      = 0.00     (below 50% M4 DoD)
```

**Result against the local code path (round-trip test, fresh DB, `recordPrDecision({fingerprint: 'a'.repeat(64)})`):**

```
total_rows = 1
fp_rows    = 1
ratio      = 1.00     (the M4 wiring is correct end-to-end)
```

### Why the prod ratio is 0%, NOT a code bug

`pr_decisions.fingerprint` is added by `TaskStore`'s constructor via a guarded `ALTER TABLE` (src/queue/store.ts:225-232). That migration runs on every `new TaskStore(...)` call. **The deployed PM2 service on the VPS has not been restarted since #312 / #316 merged**, so the live DB still carries the pre-#312 schema (no `fingerprint` column). New rows written by the running service therefore can't populate it.

**Remediation (operator action, not code):** restart the IFleet PM2 service. The ALTER will fire on next TaskStore instantiation; the column is added empty (historical rows stay NULL). The next M4-wired sprint completion will then populate fingerprints on new rows. The 50% ratio gate becomes meaningful once historical fingerprint-less rows roll out of the 30d window (or when prod accumulates ≥10 M4-era fingerprinted rows, whichever first).

### Cards generated

`reviewer_login` is also NULL on all 10 prod rows (the row producers — backfill script + M4 wiring — haven't yet been wired against a real PR reviewer source on this DB). So **0 cards are generated** for the top-3 query today.

The card pipeline itself is exercised end-to-end against synthetic data in `src/learning/reviewer-prefs/__tests__/build-cards.test.ts` (the DB-backed test that round-trips through `TaskStore`).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `pnpm test` green (428 node:test + 700 vitest, no fails)
- [x] `pnpm vitest run src/learning/reviewer-prefs src/agents/architect/tools` — 23 tests green covering: summarisation, top-N ranking, tie-break stability, fingerprint prefix bucketing, fingerprintless rows, `abandoned`→deferred mapping, disk round-trip idempotency, DB-backed window filter + top-N, `loadReviewerCard` null-on-missing, malformed-card throw, architect tool fixture round-trip, registry exposure
- [x] CLI smoke: `node --import tsx scripts/build-reviewer-cards.ts --db /tmp/empty.db --out /tmp/out` exits 0 with empty result on a fresh DB
- [x] Local M4 round-trip: `recordPrDecision({fingerprint})` reads back with fingerprint populated (proof the M4 wiring is correct in code)

## Notes for the reviewer

1. `readAllDecisions` in `build-cards.ts` reaches into the `TaskStore` private `db` handle. The `TaskStore` API doesn't expose a cross-repo decisions query today and adding one would have widened the PR (the spec says one concern). Annotated in-line. Worth a small follow-up to add `getAllPrDecisions` to the store.
2. The architect tool registry pattern was not pre-existing — `src/agents/architect/tools/` previously held one tool file (`query_code_graph.ts`) and no aggregator. The new `index.ts` is the minimum aggregator needed; M5+ planner integration may want to enrich it (signature schemas, capability descriptors).

title: AUDIT-IFleet-63037351: fix SqliteNonceLedger TOCTOU — use INSERT OR IGNORE.changes
body:
## Summary

[audit-fix:AUDIT-IFleet-63037351]

- Replace SELECT-then-INSERT pattern in `SqliteNonceLedger.registerOrReject` with a single `INSERT OR IGNORE` whose `.changes === 1` drives the return value
- Removes the multi-instance TOCTOU window: duplicate INSERT is now caught at the SQLite UNIQUE-constraint level, no pre-check needed
- Remove now-dead `selectStmt` from the class (declaration + `prepare` call)
- Add `race: two SqliteNonceLedger instances on the same DB accept a nonce exactly once` test simulating two control-plane processes sharing the same SQLite file — both call `registerOrReject('same-nonce', now)` via `Promise.all`; exactly one returns `true`
- Update `SECURITY.md` "Multi-instance regression risk" paragraph from `(known, NOT mitigated)` to `(mitigated 2026-06-04)`

## Race test methodology

The test opens two `TaskStore`/`SqliteNonceLedger` pairs on the same DB file. Both call `registerOrReject('race-test-nonce-identical', now)` with the same pinned `now` timestamp via `Promise.all`. Because better-sqlite3 is synchronous, the calls serialize within the process; the key is that the UNIQUE constraint on `nonce_ledger.nonce` is the sole arbiter — `INSERT OR IGNORE` on the second call yields `changes === 0` (row already present) so it returns `false`. The test asserts `trueCount === 1`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `node --import tsx --test src/queue/__tests__/control-plane.test.ts` — 32/32 pass (31 existing + 1 new race test)
- [x] Pre-push CI hook: full test suite green

title: feat(m4): compute structural fingerprint at teardown time (M4-T6)
body:
## Summary
- Moves `computeStructuralFingerprint` upstream from `wireSprintCompletion` (which fires AFTER the worktree is gone — so it always saw `fingerprint=NULL` in production) to `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext`'s teardown wrapper (which runs BEFORE the worktree is removed).
- Caches the hex on `TaskContextRecord.fingerprint` via a new `TaskContextRegistry.setFingerprint(taskId, hex|null)`. Resolves aliases like `setSha`.
- Simplifies `tryComputeFingerprint(ctx)` into a sync `readCachedFingerprint(ctx)` that just returns `ctx.fingerprint ?? null`. `recordPrDecisionMerged`/`recordPrDecisionRejected` become sync wrappers.
- Closes the M4 KPI gap (\"50% of merged PRs have fingerprint diff\") that T3 (#315) explicitly flagged as out-of-scope.

## Why this exists

T3-done.md from PR #315 called this out:

> Production-timing caveat. In the live pipeline, `bootstrap.teardown` (in `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext` → `teardownWorktree` in `src/pipeline/factory.ts`) runs BEFORE `task.completed` and `sprint.completed` events fire, and removes the worktree directory and deletes the local branch. That means the fingerprint compute at the `wireSprintCompletion` call site will most often see a missing `worktreePath` in production and fall back to `fingerprint=NULL`.

T3's call-site wiring shipped the correct contract (graceful null), but couldn't populate the column because the fix touches `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext` which was outside the T3 boundary. This PR fills that gap.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — clean (exit 0).
- [x] `node --import tsx --test 'src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-pr-decision.test.ts'` — 5/5 pass (updated to seed `verifierCtx.setFingerprint(taskId, hex|null)` mimicking teardown, assert wiring echoes verbatim).
- [x] `node --import tsx --test 'src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-verifier-wiring.test.ts'` — 11/11 pass including:
  - `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext caches structural fingerprint at teardown` — diverges from main, runs teardown, asserts 64-hex-char fingerprint on the registry record, AND asserts the cached hex SURVIVES `rmSync(worktree)`.
  - `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext records fingerprint=null when the worktree was already gone` — bootstrap pointed at non-existent dir, asserts `fingerprint=null` recorded, `sha` left unset.
  - `TaskContextRegistry: setFingerprint(alias, hex|null) is stored on the primary record` — alias resolution unit.
- [x] Full orchestrator suite — 116/116 pass.
- [x] Full queue suite — 131/131 pass.
- [x] Pre-push (full vitest) — 684/684 pass.

## Files touched

title: feat(classifier): M4.7 explicit category:*/severity:* label parsing
body:
## Summary

Closes ADR-0004 §Known-Limitations item 2 (M4.7). Canonical §3.2 overrides #1 (category-driven Opus) and #2 (CRITICAL-driven Opus) are now reachable via explicit `category:security|auth|payments|migration` and `severity:critical` labels in addition to the HIGH_KEYWORDS scorer. An operator who labels an issue `category:security` without putting "security" or "auth" in the title now gets the Opus promotion they expected.

## What changed

**Parser** (`src/queue/labels.ts`, `src/queue/types.ts`)

- `parseLabels` now recognises `category:*` (whitelist: `security|auth|payments|migration`) and `severity:*` (whitelist: `critical|important|cosmetic`).
- Case-insensitive matching (mirrors `model:*` / `priority:*` parsing).
- Multiple `category:*` labels: first wins.
- Unknown values: `console.warn`-logged and ignored. Labels parsing is on the hot path and must not throw on operator typos.
- `RoutingHints` type extended with optional `category?` and `severity?` fields.

**Classifier wiring** (`src/classifier/index.ts`)

The architect-tier derivation step (~lines 197-230) now consults `hints.category` and `hints.severity` BEFORE the existing scorer / `complexity:high` path. Both label-driven overrides set T2's `categoryOverrideTriggered` flag (M4.6), so a `category:auth` + `mode:tdd` combination produces architect=Opus — the flag makes T2's mode-override block skip the demotion.

Ordering inside `classifyTask`:

1. `baseTier = applyLabelBumps(scoreToTier(rawScore), hints.priority, task.labels)`
2. `architectTier = baseTier`
3. **NEW:** `if (hints.category && OPUS_CATEGORIES.has(hints.category)) → architectTier='opus'; categoryOverrideTriggered=true`
4. **NEW:** `if (hints.severity === 'critical') → architectTier='opus'; categoryOverrideTriggered=true`
5. `if (complexity === 'high') architectTier='opus'` (existing; redundant with above but harmless)

**Tests**

- `src/queue/__tests__/labels.test.ts`: case insensitivity, unknown-value graceful ignore (no throw, dev-log only), `first-wins` for multiple category labels, coexistence with existing labels.
- `src/classifier/__tests__/classifier.test.ts`: new `describe` block `classifyTask — category:*/severity:* label overrides (M4.7)` covering: label-only Opus promotion, payments category, severity:critical alone, M4.6 interaction (auth + mode:tdd → architect stays Opus), `category:unknown` ignored, label-beats-scorer (typo title + category:security → Opus).

**Docs**

- `docs/MODEL-ROUTING.md`: Label gates table extended with two new rows + explanatory note about keyword-vs-label dual-path for canonical §3.2 override detection. Override-precedence section reworded.
- `docs/adr/0004-canonical-routing-alignment.md`: §Known-Limitations item 2 prefixed with "Resolved in PR #M4.7-PENDING, 2026-06-03" (PR number to be patched in post-merge if convention requires).

## Verification

- `pnpm typecheck` → exit 0
- `pnpm lint` → exit 0
- `pnpm test` → 414 node:test pass + 648 vitest pass (1062 total), 0 fail

## Integration with T2 (M4.6 + M4.8)

T2's `categoryOverrideTriggered` flag and reviewer-derivation move (merged on main as PR #301) are the integration seam. The new label-driven triggers set the same flag, so T2's mode-override gate consistently refuses to demote any architect=Opus whose Opus status came from a canonical §3.2 override #1 or #2 source (scorer, rule, OR label).

title: fix(daemon): abort onForcePr on real push failure — AUDIT-IFleet-c9d0e1f2
body:
## Summary

Closes **AUDIT-IFleet-c9d0e1f2** (IMPORTANT). The `onForcePr` handler in `src/orchestrator/daemon.ts` previously wrapped `git push` in a try/catch that logged the failure and fell through to `octokit.rest.pulls.create` unconditionally. The inline rationale ("best-effort: pulls.create will surface a clearer error than a stale push warning") was faulty:

- For **real** push failures (auth error, network down, branch protection rejection, worktree gone), `pulls.create` then failed with a confusing 4xx and the operator's Discord follow-up said "PR may already exist" — actively misleading.
- For **benign** push successes where origin was already at the same SHA (`Everything up-to-date`), nothing was actually wrong — that case already proceeded correctly because git exits 0.

The fix classifies the push outcome into `success | already-up-to-date | failed` and gates `pulls.create` on it. On `failed`, we emit a clear `⛔ /force-pr ABORTED` message via `broadcastIFleet`, append a `verifier.force_pr_aborted` audit event next to the existing `verifier.force_pr` row, and return early — `pulls.create` is never called. On `success` or `already-up-to-date`, behaviour is unchanged.

The handler body was extracted into an exported `handleForcePr(taskId, reason, deps)` with DI seams for `execFile` and `broadcast` so the push-outcome branches can be unit-tested without booting the daemon (no subprocess, no Discord, no GitHub).

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` → exit 0
- [x] `pnpm lint` → exit 0
- [x] `pnpm test` → 394 node:test pass + 643 vitest pass (6 new tests under `daemon-force-pr.test.ts`)
- [x] New unit tests cover all four required branches:
  - push success → `pulls.create` is called
  - push `Everything up-to-date` → `pulls.create` is still called (benign)
  - push failure → `pulls.create` is NOT called, abort broadcast fires
  - push failure → `handleForcePr` resolves (does not throw)
  - push failure broadcast includes recovery guidance ("investigate push failure (auth, network, branch protection, worktree state)")
  - push failure appends `verifier.force_pr_aborted` audit event with `cause: 'push_failed'`
- [ ] CI green, mergeStateStatus CLEAN

## Canonical reference

Per `~/.claude/skills/CANONICAL-PATTERN.md` §2.7 (Fix Doctor) and §2.9 (Merge gate), this PR is AUDIT-tagged and will trigger the strict-mode tiered review chain (`/codex-review` + `verifier` subagent in parallel). Both must PASS to merge.

## Files touched

- `src/orchestrator/daemon.ts` — extracted `handleForcePr` with push-outcome classification; the inline `onForcePr` callback now delegates to it
- `src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-force-pr.test.ts` — new test file with 6 cases

title: feat(pool): wire rate_limit events into account-pool.markRateLimited (ADR-0004 §Context bullet 1)
body:
## Summary

Closes the live-wiring gap documented in `docs/adr/0004-canonical-routing-alignment.md` §Context bullet 1. Before this PR the worker `rate_limit` event was *counted* at `src/pipeline/factory.ts:165` (`rateLimitHits++`) but `account-pool.markRateLimited(id, retryAfterMs)` was never called from the live pipeline — the pool method was only exercised in unit tests. As a result, when a worker hit its 5-hour Claude Max window the fleet kept trying that worker until the OMC wait/resume wrapper caught the 429 via a separate code path.

After M4.5 Phase C removed the Phase B Opus cap, the rate-limit blast radius is real for Opus-heavy sprints. This PR closes the loop so rotation actually works in the single-seat-today / multi-seat-soon configuration.

## What changed

- **`src/pipeline/factory.ts`**
  - New exported constant `FIVE_HOURS_MS = 5 * 60 * 60 * 1000` — the Claude Max window default, used when a `rate_limit` event arrives with `retryDelayMs === 0` (terminal events sometimes omit `resetsAt`).
  - `buildWorkerPool(workerConfig, accountPool?)` now takes an **optional** `accountPool: AccountPool`. The existing call site in `makeProductionFactory` threads in the current pool reference at spawn time; the parameter is optional so `worktree-sandboxing.test.ts` and other unit-test contexts that exercise `buildWorkerPool` without a real pool keep working.
  - Inside the event-loop closure: when `event.kind === 'rate_limit'`, the handler still increments `rateLimitHits++` (observability preserved) **and** now calls `accountPool.markRateLimited(workerConfig.id, event.retryDelayMs > 0 ? event.retryDelayMs : FIVE_HOURS_MS)`. The worker id comes from the spawn-time `workerConfig` captured by the closure, so rotation skips the rate-limited account on subsequent `acquire()` calls.
- **`src/pipeline/__tests__/factory.test.ts`** — new describe block `rate_limit event wires AccountPool.markRateLimited` exercises the wiring end-to-end through a fake `PipelineWorkerAdapter` registered via `registerPipelineAdapter`. Covers: explicit retryDelayMs propagation, 5h default when 0, counter still increments, optional pool back-compat, non-rate_limit events do not invoke the pool.
- **`docs/adr/0004-canonical-routing-alignment.md`** §Context bullet 1 — prepended the resolution prefix and rewrote the closing sentence: rate-limit wiring is no longer a tracked follow-up; OMC wrapper remains the single-seat fallback for sustained windows.

## Why not change `markRateLimited`'s signature

`account-pool.markRateLimited(id, retryAfterMs)` already had the exact shape this PR needs. The worker event payload uses `retryDelayMs` (durations in ms); semantically identical. No type tweak was needed in `src/workers/account-pool.ts`.

## Verification

- `pnpm typecheck` — clean
- `pnpm lint` — clean
- `pnpm test` — node:test 388/388 pass, vitest 648/648 pass (63 files)

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green (typecheck + lint + tests)
- [ ] `/codex-review` PASS
- [ ] After merge, when a Claude Max account hits its 5h window in a live sprint, the next `acquire()` on the pool returns a different worker (observable via daemon logs / pool snapshot).

title: feat(audit): wire `verifying` status into the verifier+doctor loop
body:
## Summary

- Add `maybeMarkAuditFindingVerifying(input)` to `src/pipeline/runner.ts`, mirroring the existing `maybeCloseAuditFinding` pattern.
- Call it just before the verify+doctor loop at `runner.ts:255`. The audit-fix lifecycle is now:
  ```
  /audit-fix sets `fixing`
    → editor runs
    → runner sets `verifying`           ← NEW
    → verify + (doctor) cycles
    → markFindingClosed sets `closed`
  ```
- Mirror the status flip to Supabase via `dbUpdateFindingStatus(id, 'verifying')` so `/audit-status` on the VPS sees the in-flight state.
- Both updates are best-effort: a file or DB failure logs and continues, never unwinds the pipeline.

## Why

`AuditStatus` already lists `verifying` (SQL CHECK constraint + TS union), but no code path writes it. The dead enum slot has been sitting unused since M1 — the runner jumped `fixing → closed`, so the audit timeline couldn't distinguish "editor finished, verifier checking" from "still waiting for editor." This wires the slot and gives the timeline a real "verifier looked at this" stamp.

Step 3 of the Lane G pipeline design at `~/.claude/splits/20260601-1114-foundation-rebuild/lane-g-pipeline-design.md` §4.2 / §7.

## Behavior under failure

`setFindingsStatus` is a no-op when the finding is already terminal or already in the target state, so calling this on a non-audit task or a re-entry is safe. A finding stuck on doctor-cap failure now sits in `verifying` instead of `fixing` — same orphan today, more accurate label. **Step 4** of the build order (next PR) will reset `verifying`-or-`fixing` back to `reopened` on doctor-cap failure, closing the orphan path entirely.

## Test plan

title: feat(audit): cross-process lock on closed.json writes
body:
## Summary

- Add `src/audit/closed-json-lock.ts` — a mkdir-based cross-process lock (`withClosedJsonLock`) with stale-lock force-release.
- Wrap the critical section in `appendToClosedJson` and the closed.json branch of `markFindingsClosed` under the lock.
- 7 new unit tests in `src/audit/__tests__/closed-json-lock.test.ts` covering: acquire/release, throw-in-callback releases, stale-lock force-release, polling/serialization under contention, acquisition timeout, no leaked lock dir on success path, sequential back-to-back calls.

## Why

PR #288 shipped `appendToClosedJson` + `markFindingClosed` + `markFindingsClosed` but the critical section is a read-modify-write on `<repo>/.audits/closed.json`. With multiple processes touching that file — e.g. the standalone `/audit-fix` skill (Claude Code) running alongside the IFleet pipeline runner closing a different finding — two writers can interleave their read+append+rename and silently drop one of the records. The fingerprint-dedup inside `appendToClosedJson` doesn't protect against this because both readers see the *same* pre-write state.

This is step 2 of the Lane G pipeline design (`~/.claude/splits/20260601-1114-foundation-rebuild/lane-g-pipeline-design.md` §4.1 and §7) — the last piece that was missing after PR #288 shipped the writer itself. The closure ledger is now safe under concurrent writers.

## Lock design

- **mkdir** is the atomic acquire — guaranteed atomic on every POSIX filesystem, doesn't need `flock(1)` (which macOS lacks). Same pattern as the lane-scheduler spec.
- A held lock older than **75s** is treated as stale and force-released (the holder presumably crashed).
- Acquisition retries with **`Atomics.wait`**-based sync sleep (50ms intervals) up to a 15s deadline, then throws.
- **Sync** (not async): the callers in `pipeline/runner.ts::maybeCloseAuditFinding` are sync, and cascading async would mean ~20 file edits through the test suite for zero behavioral benefit.
- Release runs in `finally`, so a throw in the callback still releases the lock.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm typecheck` — clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` — clean
- [x] `npx vitest run src/audit/__tests__/closed-json-lock.test.ts` — 7/7 new tests pass

### Behavioral fixes

title: feat(discord): add /audit-fix slash command
body:
## Summary

Adds a Discord `/audit-fix` slash command to IFleet with three modes:

- **List** (`/audit-fix`, or `target: list`) — reads `.audits/index.json` and replies with open findings grouped by severity. No control-plane call.
- **Fix one** (`/audit-fix <AUDIT-IFleet-xxxx>`) — synthesizes a brief from the finding, submits it as a `sprint_goal`, marks the finding `fixing`.
- **Auto** (`/audit-fix auto`) — queues every open finding as its own `sprint_goal`, marks them all `fixing`.

When an audit-fix task opens a PR, the pipeline runner marks the finding `closed` with `closing_pr` set, detected via the `[audit-fix:<id>]` tag carried in the task goal.

## Key decisions

- **No `ControlCommand` variant added.** List mode is client-side; fix/auto reuse the existing `sprint_goal` command. `src/contracts/control-plane-client.ts` is untouched per the "don't add a command type if you can avoid it" guidance.
- **New `src/discord/audit-runner.ts`** holds all index read/write + brief synthesis + list formatting — kept free of discord.js / `ControlCommand` types so the pipeline runner can import the close-out helpers without a runtime coupling.
- **Path consistency:** both the Discord handler and the runner resolve `.audits/index.json` from `IFLEET_REPO_ROOT ?? process.cwd()` — identical because both run in the single IFleet daemon process.
- Findings are marked `fixing` *before* dispatch; any that fail to queue revert to `open`.
- `.audits/` added to `.gitignore` (public repo; written at runtime).

## Known follow-up (out of scope)

`reopened`-status findings are not dispatchable — Phase 4 self-healing does not produce that status yet. Revisit when fingerprint REGRESSION lands.

title: fix(pipeline): separate PR reviewer assignment from gh pr create
body:
## Summary

**F5** — the last bug blocking IFleet's pipeline from reporting success.

`buildPrOpener` ran `gh pr create ... --reviewer @monstersebas1`. The `@` prefix makes the login invalid, so `gh` **creates the PR** and then **exits non-zero** on the reviewer assignment. `execFileAsync` rejects → the task is marked `failed` even though the PR exists. Every autonomous task failed this way (observed: PR #182 created, task `01KS6MSB` marked failed).

## Fix

- `gh pr create` no longer receives `--reviewer` — the PR is created first.
- Reviewers are assigned via a separate best-effort `gh pr edit --add-reviewer`, wrapped in `.catch()` that logs and never throws. A failed review request can't fail an otherwise-successful PR.
- New `normalizeReviewers()` strips the leading `@` (config/CODEOWNERS store `@user`; `gh` wants the bare login).

## Verification

- `pnpm typecheck` clean
- Full suite: **536 vitest + node tests, 0 fail** (+1 new test file)
- Definitive verification: VPS end-to-end `/ship` run after deploy

- All 473 tests pass; `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean

## Why two files, not a runtime change?

The `claude` CLI subprocess (our current worker transport) accepts `--system-prompt <str>` but does **not** expose `cache_control` as a CLI argument. Explicit 1h TTL requires migrating from CLI subprocess to Anthropic SDK-direct calls.

**Gap filed:** SDK-direct migration is a follow-up item. Until then:
- The Anthropic auto-cache (5-min default TTL) still fires when the same static string is sent — the prompts must be bit-identical for it to hit
- This PR guarantees that invariant with tests and documents the exact cache boundary for each role

## Cache architecture (for the SDK migration PR)

| Role | Stable prefix | Dynamic suffix |
|---|---|---|
| Architect | `ARCHITECT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | `\n\n## Prior learnings\n…` (appended in `architect.ts`) |
| Editor | `EDITOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | none — fully static |
| Diff-reviewer | `REVIEWER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | none — fully static |
| Plan-reviewer | `PLAN_REVIEWER_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | none — fully static |
| Haiku gate | `HAIKU_GATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | none — fully static |
| Doctor | `DOCTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` | none — fully static |

When migrating to SDK-direct, send each constant as the FIRST content block with:
```json
{ "type": "text", "text": "<constant>", "cache_control": { "type": "ephemeral", "ttl": "1h" } }
```
Place the marker at the END of the static constant, NOT after the dynamic learnings suffix.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm test` — 473 tests pass (51 suites)
- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — no type errors
- [x] 8 new tests in `prompt-stability.test.ts` all green

title: feat(eval): A/B replay harness — baseline vs current pipeline
body:
## Summary

- New `scripts/eval-replay-ab.ts` CLI runs the eval set against two pipeline versions (baseline + current) via per-ref git worktrees, shelling out to the existing per-version runner (`scripts/eval/run-eval-replay.ts`) inside each worktree.
- Output: structured diff + pass-rate / cost-minute deltas to `.ifleet/eval/replay-results.json`.
- This is the measurement substrate that later upgrades (M3 KG, M4 PR-learning, future verifier sharpening) will benchmark against.

## CLI

```
pnpm tsx scripts/eval-replay-ab.ts \
  --baseline <git-ref> [--current <git-ref>] [--limit N] [--dry-run] \
  [--output <path>] [--worktree-root <path>] [--runner <script>]
```

## Output shape (per the T2 spec)

```json
{
  "ranAt": "...",
  "currentRef": "<sha>",
  "baselineRef": "<sha>",
  "rowCount": 2,
  "rows": [
    {
      "evalId": "ifleet-IF-107",
      "current":  { "verifierPassed": true, "costMinutes": 0, "prUrl": "..." },
      "baseline": { "verifierPassed": true, "costMinutes": 0, "prUrl": "..." },
      "diff": { "costMinutesDelta": 0, "outcomeChanged": false }
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "currentPassRate": 1, "baselinePassRate": 1,
    "currentAvgCostMinutes": 0, "baselineAvgCostMinutes": 0,
    "passRateDelta": 0, "costDeltaMinutes": 0
  }
}
```

## Design choices

- **Sibling, not replacement.** Existing `scripts/eval-replay.ts` is M1-specific (10 historical PRs through the verifier) and referenced from session memory — left untouched.
- **Per-version runner via shell-out.** Each worktree runs its own `pnpm tsx scripts/eval/run-eval-replay.ts --limit N`. Decouples the A/B harness from the pipeline internals — it can compare any two refs that ship that runner script, without pulling pipeline logic into this harness.
- **Cost minutes derived from runner `costUsd` (preferred) or `durationMs / 60000` fallback.** Single-seat Max plan → dollars are flat-rate; minutes is the meaningful axis (CLAUDE.md).
- **Worktrees NOT removed.** T1 owns cleanup after merge, per the split plan.
- **`--dry-run` skips worktree + pipeline execution** and writes a row-only plan — useful for wiring checks in CI.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm vitest run scripts/__tests__/eval-replay-ab.test.ts` — 16 tests pass (arg parsing, JSONL read with `--limit`, cost mapping, normalization, diff/summary math, ref resolution, worktree orchestration, error paths)

- [x] Smoke `--dry-run`: plan-only JSON written
- [x] Smoke real `--limit 2 --baseline HEAD~5`: both worktrees ran the per-version runner, diff JSON written to `.ifleet/eval/replay-results.json`

## Summary

Three IMPORTANT findings from the post-merge security audit of PR #164:

| # | Finding | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Server binds \`0.0.0.0\` by default → LAN-accessible despite "local-only no auth" claim | Explicit \`127.0.0.1\` bind; opt-in to LAN via \`DASHBOARD_HOST=0.0.0.0\` |
| 2 | \`/api/health\` returned \`{ ok, tasksDb, stateDb }\` leaking absolute paths | Return \`{ ok: true }\` only; new vitest regression asserts the key set |
| 3 | 500 handler forwarded raw \`Error.message\` (potentially SQLite internals, paths, stack excerpts) | Log to stderr; return generic \`internal server error\` |

## Test plan
- [x] \`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit\` — clean
- [x] \`pnpm exec vitest run dashboard\` — 10/10 (was 9; +1 regression test)
- [ ] Once merged: \`pnpm dashboard\` and confirm log line shows \`http://127.0.0.1:3737\`

## Out of scope (also audited)
- Rate limiting on \`/api/budget\` (COSMETIC for local-only use; would matter if LAN-exposed)
- GitHub-API PR titles injected via \`.innerHTML\` (COSMETIC — trusted source, attacker would need repo write)
- CORS headers (COSMETIC — opt-in via \`DASHBOARD_HOST=0.0.0.0\` is now the only way to be LAN-exposed)

---
🗣️ In plain terms: T3's dashboard PR shipped a "local-only" web server that was actually reachable from any device on your home WiFi (the default Node binding is "listen on every network interface"). It also leaked your username + db paths through the health endpoint and would forward raw SQL error messages over the wire. Three one-line fixes: bind to localhost only, strip paths from health, return a generic 500. Set \`DASHBOARD_HOST=0.0.0.0\` if you ever want it on a tablet.

title: fix(daemon): wire pipeline eventSink to StateStore.events (real close for #163)
body:
## Summary

Follow-up to PR #166. The runner already emits `reviewer.rejected` (+ 3 sibling pipeline events), but `input.eventSink` is **never set** in production wiring, so every emission lands in `undefined?.()` and is silently dropped. This PR plumbs the sink from daemon → factory wrap → runner so events actually land in the StateStore \`events\` table.

## Why

Self-audit on #163 surfaced that PR #166 satisfied the PR title but **not the issue's acceptance criterion** (\`sqlite3 ~/.omc/ifleet/state.db \"SELECT * FROM events WHERE kind='reviewer.rejected'\"\` returns 0 rows in production). Same gap silently drops:

- \`reviewer.rejected\` (PR #166 / issue #163)
- \`plan_reviewer.vetoed\` / \`plan_reviewer.escalated\` / \`plan_reviewer.skipped\`
- \`reviewer.haiku_gate_passed\` (issue #109)

One sink wire-up closes all four.

## Changes

- \`src/orchestrator/daemon.ts\`:
  - \`wrapFactoryWithApprovalAndEmit\` now accepts an optional \`emitPipelineEvent(taskId, event)\` callback and sets \`bootstrap.input.eventSink\` to a non-throwing wrapper.
  - New top-level \`persistPipelineEvent(store, taskId, event)\` translates \`PipelineEvent\` → \`OrchestratorEvent\` (resolves sprintId via \`store.loadTask\`, strips \`kind\`/\`taskId\` off the payload).
  - \`main()\` wires the callback to \`persistPipelineEvent(orchestratorStore, ...)\`.
- \`src/orchestrator/store.ts\`: new \`StateStore.loadEventsBySprint(sprintId, kind?)\` reader. The \`events\` table had only a writer; this unlocks future dashboard queries.
- \`src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-eventsink-wiring.test.ts\`: 4 new tests (wiring, backward compat, persistence, missing-task drop).

## Test plan

- [x] \`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit\` — clean
- [x] \`pnpm test\` — 360 node:test + 49 vitest test files, all green (was 356 before — 4 new tests)
- [ ] Once merged + daemon restarted: trigger a sprint that hits \`blocked_by_reviewer\` and confirm \`sqlite3 ~/.omc/ifleet/state.db \"SELECT * FROM events WHERE kind='reviewer.rejected'\"\` returns ≥1 row.

## Acceptance for #163 (re-verifying)

- [x] \`runner.ts\` emits \`reviewer.rejected\` — PR #166
- [x] **\`eventSink\` is set in production wiring — this PR**
- [x] Event lands in StateStore \`events\` table — this PR via \`persistPipelineEvent\`
- [x] Test exercises the full wiring path

## Out of scope

- Forwarding these events to Discord (separate alert-volume decision)
- Dashboard card surfacing reviewer rejections (T3 can consume \`loadEventsBySprint\` in a follow-up)
- Discord \`postFailed\` observability gap (separate PR — see #165 audit)

---
🗣️ In plain terms: PR #166 added a new event that the pipeline emits whenever a sprint dies because the reviewer rejected the diff. That event was supposed to land in the SQLite events table so you could later ask 'why did this sprint fail?'. But the pipeline emits the event into a callback (\`eventSink\`) that was never actually plugged in — so every emission has been going into the void in production since PR #166 merged. This PR plugs the callback in. As a bonus, three OTHER events that the pipeline has been emitting (around plan reviewer decisions and a cost-split gate) have ALSO been being dropped — those start persisting too. Also added a small read method so the dashboard can query the events later.

title: fix(orchestrator): Max-plan-aware budget cap (closes #162)
body:
## Background

`SprintManager.checkBudget()` (`src/orchestrator/sprint.ts:425`) pauses a sprint when the worker-reported `totalCostUsd` exceeds `BUDGET_USD`. The value comes from Claude CLI's reported cost field, which is computed from token usage at **API list prices** — regardless of how the request is actually billed.

For an Anthropic API key, that USD figure tracks real spend. For a **Max-Pro OAuth subscription** (the way IFleet authenticates today), the user pays a flat monthly fee and the per-call dollar number is meaningless. A $1 "budget" trips after ~5 sprints of ordinary work even though the user is paying $0 in marginal cost.

This has already bitten us live: prior sessions saw the alert `⚠️ Sprint <id> paused — budget $1.00 reached (spent $5.00)` fire on Max-plan workers, leaving sprints stuck in `paused` until manually resumed.

## Band-aid in place

`BUDGET_USD=0` added to `.env`. `parseBudgetEnv()` treats any non-positive value as disabled (`src/orchestrator/index.ts:384`). The cap is effectively off.

This works but is brittle: any future operator who forgets `BUDGET_USD=0` (or who sets a value thinking it tracks real spend) will hit the same trap.

## Proper fix — options

### Option A — Auth-profile-aware skip
Skip `checkBudget()` when every worker in the registry uses an OAuth/Max profile (i.e. no API key). `WorkerConfig.authProfile` already differentiates (see `src/workers/account-pool.ts` and `claude-cli` adapter `--profile` flag). If `authProfile !== 'api'` for all workers, the reported USD is not real spend → don't gate on it.

- Pros: zero ops burden, semantically correct, no env var to remember.
- Cons: needs a clear rule for mixed pools (API + Max). Probably: enforce only on API-keyed workers' attempts.

### Option B — Make cap explicitly opt-in + rename
Rename `BUDGET_USD` → `API_BUDGET_USD` and require it to be explicitly set. Document in `docs/running.md` that it only applies when at least one worker uses an API key. Default = disabled.

- Pros: simple, explicit.
- Cons: still possible to misconfigure; doesn't solve the "I set $50 thinking that was my monthly budget" footgun.

### Option C — Both
Apply the auth-profile rule (A) AND rename + opt-in (B). Belt-and-suspenders.

## Recommendation

Option A. Mixed pools are rare in practice (CLAUDE.md mandates single-seat Max-plan for now), and the auth-profile signal is already in the worker config — no new API surface. When mixed-provider rollout becomes real, extend to per-worker enforcement.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `checkBudget()` no-ops when all registered workers have `authProfile !== 'api'`
- [ ] Unit test: budget pause fires for API workers, does not fire for Max workers
- [ ] Discord alert is suppressed when budget is skipped (no spurious pause messages)
- [ ] `docs/running.md` updated to document the rule

## Out of scope

- Real cost tracking for Max plans (would require integrating Anthropic billing API, not the CLI's per-call estimate)
- Per-task spend caps (`pipeline.cost-cap` / plan-reviewer `costCapPctOfArchitect` already handle relative caps cleanly)

title: fix(pipeline): emit reviewer.rejected event with concerns (closes #163)
body:
## Symptom

When the pipeline returns `status: 'blocked_by_reviewer'` after the diff-reviewer rejects an editor's diff twice, the reviewer's verdict text and `concerns[]` array are lost. The only on-disk signal in `~/.omc/ifleet/state.db` is:

```sql
SELECT kind, payload_json FROM events WHERE sprint_id='<id>';
-- task.capability_blocked | {"exitCode":3,"error":"blocked_by_reviewer","costUsd":0}
```

No reviewer JSON output. No concerns. No raw verdict body. Operators have no way to know *why* the reviewer rejected — only that it did.

## Why this matters

Reviewer rejection is one of the most common terminal states for a sprint (observed at least 2× this week — sprints `sp_iM0HD86gRJja` and `sp_m-pfbsuNzR_m`, both `blocked_by_reviewer`). Without the concerns array we can't:
- Tell legitimate rejections from parser failures / malformed JSON from haiku
- Spot reviewer prompt drift over time
- Build a "common reviewer concerns" telemetry view
- Replay the reviewer cold to validate a fix

We're flying blind on the most-fired failure mode.

## Where the data exists today

`DefaultPipelineRunner.run` (`src/pipeline/runner.ts`) returns `PipelineResult.attempts: AttemptRecord[]`. Each `AttemptRecord` has the full `output` string (raw reviewer JSON). The result is consumed by the pipeline bridge → SprintManager, which records summary state but discards the attempts array.

## Proposed fix

- Persist the reviewer's final verdict + concerns into `events` as a `reviewer.rejected` event before returning `blocked_by_reviewer`. Payload: `{ verdict, concerns, raw, roundCount }`.
- Mirror for `plan_reviewer.vetoed` (already exists per `runner.ts:149`) and `plan_reviewer.escalated` (`runner.ts:162`) — those already fire correctly.
- Consider a dashboard view rolling these up over time.

## Out of scope

- Persisting *all* attempt outputs (would balloon DB size). Reviewer rejection is high-signal; editor/architect outputs are not.

## Discovered during

PR #161 audit (2026-05-20). The audit asked "why did the reviewer reject?" for sprint `sp_m-pfbsuNzR_m` and the answer was "you can't tell from disk."

title: fix(discord): handle non-snowflake messageId for HTTP control-plane tasks

## Summary
Today's pm2 logs surfaced two regressions in the Discord output path for HTTP-control-plane tasks (ULID messageId, not Discord snowflake):

PR #157 introduced synthetic-thread on-demand for `markPicked`. This PR closes the matching gap for `markCompleted` / `markFailed` / `markBlocked` and stops routing ULID-messageIds at `discord-thread-anchor`.

### Changes
- `channel-router-bridge.ts` — new `isDiscordSnowflake()` guard; non-snowflake messageIds fall through to channel-only routing (creates fresh anchor + thread, per Shape A in the brief).
- `queue/sources/discord.ts` — all four `markX` methods now use `resolveThread`; it returns `''` (with a structured warn) when a thread still can't be materialized, so callers no-op instead of throwing. `threadIdOrThrow` removed (unused).
