title: fix(pipeline): sandbox read-only stages in worktree + harden role prompts
body:
## Summary

Five of the six pipeline stages — architect, plan-reviewer, diff-reviewer, haiku gate, doctor — were spawning Claude subprocesses **without a `worktreePath`**, so `buildWorkerPool.spawn()` in `src/pipeline/factory.ts:130` fell back to `resolve('.')`, which is the daemon's cwd: the **host IFleet repo**. Only the editor was correctly sandboxed.

Per the handoff doc (`docs/handoff-2026-05-20-worktree-cwd-bug.md`), a test sprint in the prior session triggered the bug: the architect ran `git checkout -b feat/add-version-helper` and committed against the main repo's checkout (handoff cites SHAs `ac556db` / `b80ebe1`, sourced from that session's reflog — these were not re-verified in this PR's session since reflog entries had rolled off).

This PR closes the bug in three layers:

1. **Sandboxing.** Thread `worktreePath: input.worktreePath` from `runner.ts` into every stage's `workerPool.spawn()` call. `SpawnOpts.worktreePath` was already an optional field in `pipeline/types.ts:74-75`; the editor was the only stage using it. Now all stages do.

2. **Prompt hardening.** Append explicit `ABSOLUTE RULES — do not use Edit/Write/Bash, do not run git, do not create or modify files` guards to `ARCHITECT_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `REVIEWER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `PLAN_REVIEWER_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `HAIKU_GATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `DOCTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT`, and `INTERVIEW_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (added in follow-up commit). These six roles are read-only by intent; the prompt now states it.

3. **Fail-loud spawn boundary.** `buildWorkerPool.spawn()` throws when `opts.worktreePath` is missing instead of silently falling back to `process.cwd()`. The bug class cannot regress through this seam.

## Live verification (this session)

Sent a fresh test task (`01KS4WTEST000000002IFLEET`), then ran `lsof` against the architect subprocess (parent = daemon pid 97651):

```
COMMAND   PID USER   FD   TYPE   DEVICE  SIZE/OFF   NODE NAME
2.1.145 98243  Seb   cwd   DIR     1,15      1056  …  /Users/Seb/dev/IFleet/.omc/worktrees/task-01KS4WTEST000000002IFLEET
```

Architect cwd is the per-task worktree, not `/Users/Seb/dev/IFleet`. Reviewer subprocess (pid 6144) also verified in worktree cwd. Main repo `git status` stayed on `fix/pipeline-worktree-sandboxing` throughout the test sprint — no branch switches, no rogue commits. The sprint ended `blocked_by_reviewer` (reviewer rejected the editor's diff twice — separate concern, not regression).

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/pipeline/architect.ts` | `worktreePath?: string` added to `RunArchitectInput`, threaded into both spawn calls (planning + interview branch) |
| `src/pipeline/plan-reviewer.ts` | Same — `worktreePath` field + spawn-opts threading |
| `src/pipeline/diff-reviewer.ts` | Same — for full reviewer + internal haiku gate |
| `src/pipeline/doctor.ts` | Same |
| `src/pipeline/runner.ts` | Pass `worktreePath: input.worktreePath` into all four stage calls; architect log line now includes the worktree |
| `src/pipeline/prompts.ts` | Append read-only guards to five system prompts |
| `src/pipeline/interview.ts` | Append read-only guard to `INTERVIEW_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (audit follow-up) |
| `src/pipeline/factory.ts` | Throw in `buildWorkerPool.spawn` when `worktreePath` is missing; export `buildWorkerPool` for testability (audit follow-up) |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/worktree-sandboxing.test.ts` | New file — 8 tests covering the fail-loud guard + spawn-opts threading per stage (audit follow-up) |

Editor (`src/pipeline/editor.ts`) was already correct — no change.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` — 0 errors
- [x] `pnpm test` — **432 vitest** (+8 new regression tests) + 355 node, no regressions
- [x] Live sprint with `lsof` confirms architect cwd = sandboxed worktree
- [x] Reviewer subprocess also verified in worktree cwd
- [x] Main repo reflog stayed clean across the test sprint
- [x] CI green on this PR

## Audit follow-ups still open

A self-audit after the first commit surfaced a few items not blocking this PR but worth tracking:

- **Reviewer concerns aren't persisted** — when the runner returns `blocked_by_reviewer`, the verdict text and concerns array stay in-memory and are lost when the pipeline exits. Today the only on-disk signal is `task.capability_blocked` with `exitCode:3`. Worth filing a small issue.
- **`node_modules` symlink + `repoRoot` arg** still let read-only host access — sandboxing is partial, not absolute. Not introduced by this PR.

## Risk

Low. All input-type additions are optional fields. Prompt changes are additive. Editor behavior unchanged. The new fail-loud guard in `buildWorkerPool.spawn` is at a seam that production code paths already populate; tests don't go through it.

title: fix(daemon): call recoverStale() at boot and cancel stale running sprints
body:
## Summary

- **`recoverStale()` at boot**: was only called in `server.ts`, not `daemon.ts` — tasks stuck `in_flight` from a crash were never reset on boot
- **Double-dispatch prevention**: after `recoverStale()` resets a task to `pending`, if StateStore still has a `running` sprint for it, `resumeAbandoned()` recovers the old sprint while the tick loop creates a second → two architect subprocesses per task. Fixed by cancelling all `running` StateStore sprints before the `Orchestrator` constructor runs
- Regression test added: verifies that after the boot-time sprint cancellation, `resumeAbandoned()` finds nothing to recover

## Root cause (from handoff-2026-05-20.md §3)

```
Orchestrator constructor → resumeAbandoned() loads running sprints → adds to activeSprintIds
recoverStale() resets in_flight task to pending
pickNext() returns the now-pending task
runTickLoop → submitSprint() → second sprint created
Both sprints tick → two architects dispatched
```

## Test plan
- [ ] 424 tests passing (+ 1 new regression test)
- [ ] Typecheck clean (`pnpm tsc --noEmit`)

title: fix(test): daemon-verifier-wiring no longer contaminates host git config
body:
## Root cause

`src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-verifier-wiring.test.ts` spawned git via `execFileAsync('git', ['-C', worktree, ...])` while inheriting the parent process env. **When the test suite runs from the `git push` pre-push hook, git sets `GIT_DIR` / `GIT_WORK_TREE` in the hook environment.** Those env vars override repo discovery — `-C <path>` changes the working dir, but git still consults `GIT_DIR` first. So every `git config user.email test@test` invocation wrote to the **host** repo's `.git/config` instead of the tmpdir, producing bogus `test@test` commits on the active branch that got force-pushed alongside real work (see PR #134 incident).

Of the four hypotheses in the bug memo (`bug_prepush_test_contamination.md`):

| # | Hypothesis | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Vitest worker cwd race | ❌ Wrong. Test uses `node:test`, not vitest; bug also reproduces with `node:test` alone under hook env |
| 2 | `-C` silently ignored | ❌ Not silent — overridden by env, but only when `GIT_DIR` is set |
| 3 | `--global` masquerade | ⚠️ Close — actually it's `GIT_DIR` env masquerading as local-but-host |
| 4 | Test inadvertently invokes host git | ✅ **Correct, via inherited GIT_DIR env from the pre-push hook** |

## Repro (pre-fix)

```bash
# From inside the IFleet repo, simulating the pre-push hook env:
GIT_DIR=$PWD/.git GIT_WORK_TREE=$PWD node -e "$(cat <<'JS'
const { execFile } = require('child_process');
const { mkdtempSync } = require('fs');
const { tmpdir } = require('os');
const { join } = require('path');
const { promisify } = require('util');
const execFileAsync = promisify(execFile);
(async () => {
  const wt = join(mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), 'repro-')), 'wt');
  await execFileAsync('git', ['init', '-q', '-b', 'main', wt]);
  await execFileAsync('git', ['-C', wt, 'config', 'user.email', 'test@test']);
})();
JS
)"
git config user.email   # → test@test (host repo contaminated!)
```

## Fix

Route every git call through a helper that strips `GIT_*` env vars before spawning git. This matches the pattern already in `src/repos/__tests__/manager.test.ts:14-17` (the comment there is literally "Strip git hook env vars so spawned processes use cwd for repo discovery"). Minimal surgical change — 16 insertions, 4 deletions.

## Why this prevents recurrence

With `GIT_*` env vars stripped, git's repo discovery falls back to its normal sequence: read the current working directory (set via `-C <path>`), walk up looking for `.git/`. The tmpdir contains a fresh `.git/`, so writes land there. There is no longer a path by which the test can touch the host repo's config.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean
- `pnpm exec eslint` clean on touched file
- `pnpm test` green (345 node tests + 344 vitest tests = 689 total)
- `pnpm test` with `GIT_DIR=$PWD/.git` set (simulated hook env): green AND host `.git/config` gains no `[user]` section
- **Real `git push` of this branch:** pre-push hook ran the full suite, host `.git/config` stayed clean, no bogus `test@test` commits appeared

## Notes

- One commented line of context explaining the `cleanGitEnv` constant — not removable without confusing future readers since the why is non-obvious from the code itself.
- No new dependencies (`simple-git` not introduced; the in-band fix is sufficient).
- No singleFork brute-force fallback needed (would have halved test parallelism).

Closes the contamination side of task #24.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: feat(ci): anti-fabrication harness — validate doc tables vs data files
body:
## Summary
- Adds `scripts/validate-claims.ts` — generic markdown-table-vs-JSON validator. Walks `<!-- claim:TYPE src="PATH" -->...<!-- /claim -->` blocks, validates rows against the cited file via a per-claim-type registry (starter handlers: `replay-results`, `verifier-baseline`). Tolerant value matching with file:line + expected/found error reporting.
- Adds `.github/workflows/validate-claims.yml` — CI gate that runs on every PR touching `.md`. Scoped to changed claim blocks via `git diff --name-only ${base}...HEAD -- '*.md'`, so pre-existing violations in unrelated files do not block unrelated PRs.
- Adds `docs/elevation/anti-fabrication-audit.md` — current-main audit findings (see below).
- 16 unit tests covering parser, validator, normalization, discovery, code-fence skipping, and registry extensibility.

## Motivation
PR #128 shipped a results table in `docs/elevation/upgrades/01-verifier.md` reporting "9/10 passed, DoD gate PASSED, disagreementRate 0.100" while the JSON it cited (`.ifleet/eval/replay-results.json`) reported `passedCount: 0`, `disagreementRate: null`, and every task at `status: "error"`. PR #129 corrects the descriptive framing but does not replace the contradictory tables. A reviewer pass would have caught the gap; it did not. This change adds the systemic check so the next reviewer pass has a robot doing the read-the-JSON step.

## How claims are expressed

```markdown
<!-- claim:replay-results src=".ifleet/eval/replay-results.json" -->
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Pass rate | 0 / 10 (0%) |
| DoD gate (≥ 8 / 10) | ✗ FAILED |
| disagreementRate | null |
| Avg duration per run | 2457 ms |
| Total cost | $0.00 |
<!-- /claim -->
```

A mismatch produces:

```
ERROR docs/.../01-verifier.md:172
  claim:replay-results src=".ifleet/eval/replay-results.json"
  row "Pass rate":
    expected (passedCount / totalCount): 0 / 10 (0%)
    found:                                **9 / 10 (90%)**
```

Unknown claim types, missing source files, and malformed JSON are hard errors. Unknown row labels and missing expected rows are warnings (informational, do not block merge).

## Registry
- `replay-results` — `passedCount`, `totalCount`, `passRatePct`, `dodGatePassed`, `disagreementRate`, `avgDurationMs`, `totalCostUsd`, `sandboxMode`, `eventsEmitted` from `scripts/eval-replay.ts` output. Tolerant matching (e.g. `9 / 10` ≡ `9/10` ≡ `9 / 10 (90%)`; `✓ PASSED` ≡ `passed` ≡ `yes`; integer-percentage rounding tolerated; duration unit s↔ms; cost `$0.00` ≡ `0`).
- `verifier-baseline` — `disagreementRate`, `falsePositiveRate`. Reserved for future M2+ baseline files.
- New types are registered via `registerClaimType(name, handler)`.

## CLI
- `pnpm validate-claims` — scan repo, exit 1 on error findings.
- `pnpm validate-claims --file <path>` — single doc.
- `pnpm validate-claims --fix-suggestions` — print proposed corrected tables (does not auto-edit).
- `pnpm validate-claims --changed-only --base <ref>` — used by CI to scope to the PR's `.md` changes.

## Audit findings (current `main`)
- **2 claim-shaped tables found**, both in `docs/elevation/upgrades/01-verifier.md` (lines 170–176 summary, 180–191 per-task breakdown).
- **2 violations** — both contradict `.ifleet/eval/replay-results.json`. PR #129 (still open) describes the root cause but does not rewrite the tables.
- **0 correct** — no doc on main used claim blocks before this PR.
- No claim-shaped violations in any other `docs/elevation/**` or `.ifleet/**` file.

Full details (proposed corrections, follow-up items, relationship to PR #129) in `docs/elevation/anti-fabrication-audit.md`.

## Scope discipline
This PR does **not**:
- Rewrite the existing fabrication in `01-verifier.md` (separate PR or amendment to #129).
- Block merges of unrelated PRs (CI is scoped to changed `.md` files in the PR diff).
- Try to validate non-markdown claims (PR descriptions, commit messages) — out of scope.

## Files
- `scripts/validate-claims.ts` (new, ~530 lines)
- `scripts/__tests__/validate-claims.test.ts` (new, 16 tests)
- `.github/workflows/validate-claims.yml` (new)
- `docs/elevation/anti-fabrication-audit.md` (new)
- `package.json` (`validate-claims` script)

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `pnpm exec eslint scripts/validate-claims.ts scripts/__tests__/validate-claims.test.ts` — clean
- [x] `pnpm test` — 359 → 360 tests passing (+15 new unit tests, +1 fence-skipping test)
- [x] `pnpm validate-claims` on current `main` — 0 blocks scanned, exits 0 (no false positives; pre-existing fabrications are not yet wrapped in claim blocks and would be wrapped by the follow-up correction PR)
- [x] Smoke-tested against a synthetic doc with one valid block (matches data → exits 0) and one invalid block reproducing PR #128's fabrication (Pass rate / DoD gate / disagreementRate → 3 mismatch errors, exit 1)
- [x] Code-fenced example claim blocks in `anti-fabrication-audit.md` are correctly ignored

---
🗣️ In plain terms:

A doc in PR #128 had a results table that said "9 out of 10 tasks passed" while the JSON data file right next to it said "0 passed, everything errored." Nobody — me, the reviewer, CI — caught the contradiction before merge. PR #129 explains what really happened in words, but the wrong-numbers table is still sitting there.

This PR adds a robot reviewer. Authors wrap any table that summarizes a JSON/data file in a special HTML-comment marker (`<!-- claim:TYPE src="path/to/data.json" -->`). When a PR touches those files, GitHub Actions opens the JSON, reads each table row, and fails the build if the numbers don't match — pointing exactly at the line that lies. It does not auto-rewrite anything, just blocks the merge with a clear error.

Three things ship: the validator script, the CI gate, and an audit doc that lists which existing tables on `main` are currently lying (answer: just the two in `01-verifier.md` that PR #128 introduced). The fix for those is left to a follow-up PR so this one stays focused.

## Summary
- `DockerSandboxRunner.run()` now clones `repoUrl` into a temp dir and checks out `sha` when `worktreePath` is not provided, instead of returning `error: "clone-from-SHA not yet supported"`.
- Temp dir is removed in a `finally` block — cleanup runs even when verification throws.
- Existing worktree-mount path is unchanged; only the missing-worktree branch is new.

## Why
Unblocks M1.W3 / IFleet P2: `/verify <taskId>` reruns that happen after the worktree was torn down now work, and the M1 DoD task (replay 10 eval-set tasks) can proceed without re-running architect/editor.

## Implementation notes
- `cloneFromSha(repoUrl, sha)` uses `git clone --quiet` + `git -C <dir> checkout --quiet <sha>` via the same injectable `spawnFn` the rest of the runner uses, so unit tests stay hermetic.
- Failure surfaces as `status: 'error'` with an `install`-kind failure carrying the trimmed git stderr — same shape callers already handle.
- Refactored the post-validation phase loop into a `runInWorktree(...)` private method so the cleanup `try/finally` only wraps what it owns.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm typecheck`
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/verifier` — 38/38 pass
- [x] Updated `returns error when worktreePath is missing` test to verify clone-failure path
- [x] Added `clones from SHA when worktreePath is not provided` test asserting `git clone <repoUrl>` + `git checkout <sha>` invocations

🗣️ In plain terms:
Before this PR, the verifier (the agent that runs build/typecheck/lint/test on a worker's code) refused to do its job unless someone handed it an already-checked-out folder. That's fine during a normal sprint, but it broke any rerun where the folder had been cleaned up. Now the verifier can clone the repo itself, jump to the exact commit it's supposed to verify, run the checks, and tidy up after. Net effect: the M1 milestone — replaying 10 eval-set tasks to measure our pass rate — can finally run.

title: feat(daemon): wire VerifierController to task.completed events
body:
## Summary

Attaches `VerifierController` to the orchestrator event bus inside `daemon.ts` so the real Docker sandbox (M1.W2) runs on every production `task.completed` event — **without touching `src/orchestrator/sprint.ts`** (per the M1 T2 boundary narrowing).

Closes the P1 _Wire verifier into daemon_ item in `~/.claude/projects/-Users-Seb/memory/task-queue.md`. Unblocks the M1 DoD task (replay 10 eval-set tasks → ≥8 must pass the verifier).

## What changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/orchestrator/daemon.ts` | +93 LOC of code: `TaskContextRegistry`, `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext`, `resolveVerifierContext`, and the `VerifierController` instantiation + `orchestrator.on('event', controller.onEvent)`. |
| `src/orchestrator/__tests__/daemon-verifier-wiring.test.ts` | New `node:test` suite (3 tests) covering bootstrap → teardown → resolve with a real git repo + synthetic `task.completed` event. |

## How the resolver gets repoUrl/branch/sha/worktreePath

- `wrapFactoryWithVerifierContext` records `repoUrl`/`branch`/`worktreePath` at bootstrap from the bridge brief + `titleToBranchName`.
- The wrapper intercepts `bootstrap.teardown` to capture HEAD SHA via `git rev-parse HEAD` **before** the production teardown removes the worktree (which happens before `task.completed` fires).
- `resolveVerifierContext` looks up `sprintId` from `StateStore` and returns a fully-populated `TaskRunContext` (or `null` if SHA capture failed — verifier then skips gracefully).

## Size note

Spec called for ~30 LOC with an 80 LOC cap before an ADR. Final: **+93 LOC of code**. No ADR added — the architecture is exactly what the M1.W3 brief described (thin external subscriber, zero `sprint.ts` touch); the extra lines are bookkeeping (SHA capture in teardown, registry class) rather than a new architectural decision. Happy to compress further if reviewer disagrees.

## Quality gate
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`: ✅ clean
- `pnpm exec eslint`: ✅ clean
- `pnpm test`: ✅ **688 tests pass** (345 node:test + 343 vitest), 0 fail

- [ ] After merge, deploy to VPS and tail `pm2 logs ifleet` while submitting a sprint — expect `verifier.started` / `verifier.passed|failed` rows in the events table
- [ ] Then proceed to the next queued item: _M1 DoD: replay 10 eval-set tasks_

---

🗣️ In plain terms:

The closed-loop verifier (Docker sandbox that re-runs tests and lint on the editor's output) now actually fires on every finished task instead of just sitting there. The hard part was that the worktree gets deleted right before the task is marked complete, so we had to sneak in a step to grab the commit SHA before deletion. Then VerifierController can use that SHA later to run its checks. Nothing about how sprints work changed — the verifier is bolted on as a separate listener.

title: feat(classifier): sprint mode routing + Haiku auto-router
body:
## Problem

All sprints use the same system prompt and retry policy regardless of task type. A persistence task needs different instructions than a test-driven task. There is no way to signal intent without writing it into the brief body.

## Solution

Extend the routing system to support sprint modes via GitHub issue labels. Four modes, each mapped to a distinct label:

| Label | Mode | Behavior |
|-------|------|----------|
| `mode:ralph` | Persistence | Editor prompt adds: "Do not stop until the task is fully complete. If CI fails, fix it. If the reviewer rejects, address every concern. Max 5 retry rounds." |
| `mode:ulw` | Speed | Architect plans in bullet points only (no prose). Editor makes one focused change per commit. |
| `mode:tdd` | Test-first | Architect plan must list failing tests to write FIRST, then implementation. Editor writes tests before touching implementation files. |
| `mode:deslop` | Cleanup | Editor prompt adds: "Remove all unnecessary complexity, dead code, unused imports. No new features. Leave the code cleaner than you found it." |

If no `mode:*` label is present, behavior is unchanged (default mode — current behavior).

## Implementation

### Label parsing
Extend `parseLabels` (or equivalent) to extract the mode from the issue's label set and return one of: `ralph | ulw | tdd | deslop | default`.

### Prompt injection
Each mode only changes the system prompt injected into the architect and/or editor — no new pipeline stages, no new agents.

### Threading
Mode must flow: `parseLabels` → routing decision → pipeline input → prompt construction. It must not be inferred from the brief body.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each criterion must be independently testable:

1. **Label → mode mapping** — `parseLabels` returns `ralph` for `mode:ralph`, `ulw` for `mode:ulw`, `tdd` for `mode:tdd`, `deslop` for `mode:deslop`
2. **Distinct prompts** — each of the 4 modes produces a different effective system prompt string compared to default and to each other
3. **Default mode** — absence of any `mode:*` label results in `default` mode with no system prompt change
4. **Thread integrity** — the mode value is correctly passed from label parsing through to the final prompt construction step

## Out of Scope

- Combining multiple modes on a single sprint
- Dynamic mode switching mid-sprint
- New pipeline stages or agents for any mode
- A `mode:` label for the default/no-op case

title: feat(pipeline): per-repo learnings + deep-interview phase in architect
body:
## Problem

IFleet has no memory across sprints. Each sprint starts cold. Repeated mistakes are never learned — the architect makes the same wrong assumptions sprint after sprint with no way to improve.

## Solution

A lightweight learnings file at `<worktree>/.ifleet/learnings.md` (created if missing). The architect reads it before planning and the failure handler appends a short lesson after each sprint failure.

## Implementation

### Architect phase change
Before generating the plan, read `.ifleet/learnings.md` from the worktree root. If the file exists and is non-empty, append a section to the architect system prompt:

```
## Repo learnings
{contents of .ifleet/learnings.md}
```

If the file is missing or empty, no change to the prompt.

### Failure handler change
On `task.failed` event: append a new line to `.ifleet/learnings.md` in the worktree:

```
- [ISO date] {failureReason}
```

If the file does not exist, create it with that line as the first entry. After writing, commit and push the file from the worktree.

## Acceptance Criteria

Each criterion must be independently testable:

1. **Learnings injected** — when `.ifleet/learnings.md` is non-empty, the architect system prompt includes the `## Repo learnings` section with the file contents
2. **Learnings omitted** — when `.ifleet/learnings.md` is missing or empty, the architect system prompt has no learnings section
3. **Failure appends** — when a sprint fails, a new lesson line is appended to `.ifleet/learnings.md`
4. **File created on first failure** — if `.ifleet/learnings.md` does not exist, the failure handler creates it with the lesson as the first line

## Out of Scope

- RAG or vector search over learnings
- Cross-repo sharing of learnings
- UI for viewing or editing learnings
- Summarizing or compressing old learnings

title: feat(pipeline): doctor extension — fingerprints, Haiku scan, daily rollup
body:
## Behavior

The doctor gains three new cadences beyond its current on-demand CI-fix role:

1. **Real-time fingerprint match** — before any LLM call, the CI log is matched against a table of known failure signatures. A match immediately applies a recovery strategy (escalate model, retry clean, double timeout) without spending tokens.

2. **Periodic Haiku scan (15 min)** — a background `DoctorScheduler` reads recent failure events and asks Haiku to identify emerging patterns. Uses its own dedicated single-worker Haiku pool so scans cannot starve active tasks.

3. **Daily rollup (midnight)** — aggregates the day's failure patterns and appends a dated section to `<repo>/.ifleet/learnings.md` so the auto-router (#75) can load them on the next sprint.

Five new recovery strategies:
- Architect fails twice → escalate to opus
- Reviewer rejects twice for the same reason → bump model + flip provider
- Verify timeout → double the timeout and retry once
- Worktree allocation conflict → prune and retry
- Doctor cap hit → post Discord message (✅ = skip, ❌ = fail, 30-min silence = fail)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A CI log containing `"Cannot find module"` or `"TS2307"` matches the `tsc-no-module` fingerprint
- [ ] Fingerprint match is applied before any Haiku/runDoctor call (verified via attempt count in tests)
- [ ] Two architect failures escalate the architect spec to opus and retry; already-at-opus → fail with `'architect failed at max tier'`
- [ ] Reviewer concerns repeated across two rounds (first 40 chars overlap) trigger model escalation + provider flip
- [ ] A verify timeout doubles once; a second timeout fails the task normally
- [ ] Doctor cap hit **with** `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` configured: posts Discord message with ✅ / ❌ instructions, polls for reaction, 30-min timeout auto-fails
- [ ] Doctor cap hit **without** Discord URL: preserves current fail-immediately behavior
- [ ] `DoctorScheduler.runScan()` fires every 15 min, emits `doctor.scan_report` event, writes `.omc/sprints/doctor-scan-latest.json`
- [ ] Daily rollup fires at midnight (wall clock), appends `## YYYY-MM-DD` section to `.ifleet/learnings.md` without overwriting existing content
- [ ] Rollup creates `.ifleet/learnings.md` if it does not exist
- [ ] All strategies have per-task retry caps — no infinite loops possible

## Implementation notes

**New files:**

| File | Purpose | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/pipeline/fingerprints.ts` | Schema, 5 built-in fingerprints, `matchFingerprint()` | +80 |
| `src/pipeline/recovery-strategies.ts` | Strategies 1/2/3/5/7 implementations | +120 |
| `src/pipeline/doctor-scheduler.ts` | `DoctorScheduler` class — scan + rollup | +150 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/fingerprints.test.ts` | Fingerprint match tests | +40 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/recovery.test.ts` | Strategy unit tests | +80 |
| `src/pipeline/__tests__/doctor-scheduler.test.ts` | Scan + rollup tests (mock workerPool) | +60 |

**Modified files:**

| File | Change | ~Lines |
|---|---|---|
| `src/pipeline/runner.ts` | Fingerprint check before doctor, strategy hooks, cap-hit handler | +60 |
| `src/pipeline/types.ts` | Add `verifyTimeoutMs?`, `verifyDoubled?` to `PipelineInput` | +5 |
| `src/orchestrator/index.ts` | Instantiate `DoctorScheduler`, pass `eventLog`, wire Discord URL and dedicated scanner pool | +30 |

Total: ~625 lines net new.

**Built-in fingerprints (v1 seed):**

| id | patterns | strategy |
|---|---|---|
| `tsc-no-module` | `"Cannot find module"`, `"TS2307"` | `escalate_model` |
| `esm-require` | `"ERR_REQUIRE_ESM"`, `"Cannot use import statement"` | `escalate_model` |
| `lint-fail` | `"ESLint"`, `"lint failed"` | `escalate_model` |
| `test-timeout` | `"Timeout"`, `"exceeded"`, `"jest --testTimeout"` | `double_timeout` |
| `worktree-conflict` | `"worktree already exists"`, `"already locked"` | `retry_clean` |

**Scanner pool (Q5):** Dedicated single-worker Haiku pool wired via `OrchestratorOptions.scannerWorkerPool?: WorkerPool`. Omitting it disables the scheduler cleanly.

**Rollup timing (Q4):** `DoctorScheduler` calculates ms until next 00:00 local time on start, schedules first rollup with `setTimeout`, repeats with `setInterval` every 24 h.

**Doctor cap Discord gate (Q3):** Polls Discord message for `✅`/`+1`/`rocket`/`hooray` (skip) or `❌` (fail). 30-min no-reaction → auto-fail.

## Out of scope

- Worktree prune implementation (belongs in `PipelineRunnerFactory`, separate issue)
- Extending fingerprint table beyond v1 seed set
- Strategy 2 `allowProviderFlip` field (can be revisited if cross-provider rule conflicts)

## Dependencies

- Soft dependency on #69 (3a — learnings.md path) for rollup destination
- Soft dependency on #75 (4a — auto-router) to consume what rollup writes; can land independently

title: fix(scripts): single-dispatcher lock to prevent duplicate sprint dispatch
body:
## Summary

Adds a process-level lockfile at `.omc/dispatcher.lock` to fence concurrent `run-smoke.ts` invocations. Fixes the duplicate-sprint-dispatch bug observed in production tonight.

## Symptom

Five sprints emitted `▶ Sprint started: #N` twice within 500–1000ms windows in #ifleet between 20:05 and 21:55 UTC on 2026-05-15. Always within a single second-window aligned to the `*/5 * * * *` cron tick (#70, #72, #90, #69, #76).

## Root cause

`GitHubQueue.pickNext` (`src/queue/github.ts:127`) is **not atomic**: two concurrent dispatchers both read the label list **before** either has called `markPicked`, so both pick the same issue and both notify. Classic TOCTOU. Concurrent dispatchers triggered by PM2 `cron_restart` overlap or any other trigger (future MCP `submitSprint` from #70, manual run, webhook).

## Fix

Atomic `O_EXCL` lockfile at `.omc/dispatcher.lock`, acquired before any GitHub I/O:

- Writes dispatcher PID; refuses to start if another **live** PID holds it (exits 0 quietly)
- Reclaims **stale** locks: holder PID dead OR file mtime > 30min
- Idempotent release; refuses to delete a lock reclaimed by another PID
- Cleanup on SIGINT/SIGTERM

## Diff

- `scripts/dispatcher-lock.ts` — new module, no external deps
- `scripts/run-smoke.ts` — wraps `main` body in lock acquire/release
- `scripts/__tests__/dispatcher-lock.test.ts` — 7 vitest cases

## Verification

- tsc clean, eslint clean
- vitest **143/143 green** (136 prior + 7 new)
- husky pre-push verde

## Follow-up worth filing

Make `pickNext` itself atomic at the queue layer (compare-and-swap via issue assignment, or DB lock). Lockfile is belt-and-braces for now.

## Operational note

After merge, `pm2 restart ifleet`. Overlapping triggers will then exit 0 instead of duplicating sprints.

title: fix(orchestrator): persist sprint spend + auto-loop tick on task completion
body:
**Depends on #85** (Lote B) — merge that first; this PR's diff will shrink once #85 is in main. The cost numbers this PR persists come from `SpawnResult.totalCostUsd`, which #85 plumbs through `architect/editor/doctor/reviewer` and into the `AttemptRecord`.

## Why

Two related holes in the orchestrator that together made the budget guard cosmetic and added ~5 minutes of dead air between task dispatches:

**C-3 — runtime counters were in-memory only.** `SprintManager.sprintSpend` (Map<SprintId, number>) and `rateLimitResetAt` (Map<SprintId, number>) lived only on the class instance. The PM2 cron bounces the host process every 5 minutes (see `docs/running.md`), so every restart blew both Maps away. Net: `BUDGET_USD=5.00` reset to `$0` at every cron tick, so a sprint that legitimately hit the cap got another `$BUDGET_USD` of free runway after the next bounce. The rate-cap pause had the same shape — a crash while paused forgot when to auto-resume, and the sprint sat stranded until an operator nudged it.

**I-4 — `tick()` only ran on the external cron.** `awaitHandle` settled a finished task and… stopped. The next task on the same sprint waited for the cron's next scheduled call (up to 5 min) before being dispatched, even when a worker had just freed up. For a 5-task sprint that translated to ~25 minutes of pure idle time.

## What

- New table `sprint_runtime_state` (`sprint_id` PK, `spent_usd` REAL, `rate_reset_at` INTEGER, `updated_at` INTEGER) added to `MIGRATIONS` in `src/orchestrator/store.ts`. Additive — the existing `sprints` row + state-machine blob are untouched, so older DBs migrate without touching live data.
- New `StateStore` methods `saveSprintSpend`, `saveSprintRateReset`, `loadAllSprintRuntime`. The two save methods are upserts that preserve the column they don't touch (so persisting spend does not clobber a paused-rate timestamp, and vice versa).
- `SprintManager` constructor now rehydrates both Maps from `loadAllSprintRuntime()` on boot. Restart-safe.
- `accumulateCost` upserts the running total on every credited attempt. `checkRateLimit` upserts the `resetAt` on every pause. The branch in `tick()` that detects the window has opened clears `rate_reset_at` to NULL so a subsequent restart cannot re-pause on a stale timestamp.
- `awaitHandle` now ends with `this.scheduleTick(entry.sprintId)`. `scheduleTick` is a tiny private helper: reads the sprint, bails on terminal or paused state (so a budget pause does **not** trigger an immediate re-dispatch), and otherwise `setImmediate(() => this.tick(...).catch(() => undefined))`. Errors are swallowed because the external cron will retry; surfacing them in `awaitHandle` would just clobber the original task-completion event.

## Tests (`src/orchestrator/__tests__/launch-hardening.test.ts`, node:test)

- **Persistence smoke**: phase 1 runs a sprint with `totalCostUsd=1.5`, tears down the SprintManager + StateStore. Phase 2 opens a fresh StateStore on the same DB, calls `loadAllSprintRuntime()`, asserts `spent_usd === 1.5`, and constructs a new SprintManager (which rehydrates) without throwing.
- **Auto-loop happy path**: two tasks, single worker, **one** explicit `tick()`. Asserts both `adapter.spawned` entries land — only possible if `awaitHandle` re-ticked on its own.
- **Auto-loop pause guard**: budget=1.0, first task reports cost 5.0 so the budget pause fires after task 1. Asserts the second task is NOT auto-dispatched — the `scheduleTick` short-circuit on paused state holds.

These cases live in `launch-hardening.test.ts` (node:test, the existing pattern for this file). They do not run under the husky pre-push hook on Windows because the glob `'src/orchestrator/**/*.test.ts'` does not expand in the Windows shell — a preexisting limitation that also bit #84 / #85 / #86. CI on Linux/Mac will execute them.

## Local verification

`pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean. `pnpm exec eslint .` clean (the single pre-existing `no-console` warning in `pipeline/runner.ts:161` is unchanged — comes from #85's surface, not this PR). `pnpm test` reports `129/129` vitest green; husky pre-push hook ran on push and passed.

## Incidental

Branch is based on `fix/budget-cost-propagation` (PR #85) and carries the same `970aab0` cherry-pick of the Windows `screenshot.test.ts` fix that #84 / #85 / #86 use to keep husky pre-push from blocking on Windows. No-op on rebase once any of those land.

## Out of scope (other batches in the stabilization sprint)

- C-1 author allowlist (#84)
- C-2 budget cost propagation (#85) — **prerequisite for this PR**
- I-5 + I-7 workers/claude.ts cleanup (#86)
- I-6 + I-8 run-smoke ESM + Windows junction (#87)

## Operator checklist before merge

- [ ] Merge #85 first — without `SpawnResult.totalCostUsd` carrying real numbers, `accumulateCost` persists `0` on every attempt and this PR's behavior collapses back to "decorative".
- [ ] After the first sprint that hits a PM2 restart, confirm `sprint_runtime_state` in `~/.omc/ifleet/state.db` carries the running total and that the next sprint's first tick respects the cap.
- [ ] Watch for any sprint that auto-loops faster than expected: if a worker pool reports rate-cap before `awaitHandle` settles, `scheduleTick` should still bail on `paused`. The pause-guard test covers this, but production behavior under real rate-limit pressure is worth a manual sanity-check.

title: fix(scripts): ESM-correct https import + Windows-safe worktree symlink in run-smoke
body:
**Depends on #85** (Lote B — budget cost propagation). Merge that first; this PR's diff will shrink to only the two changes below once #85 is in `main`.

## Why

Two latent defects in `scripts/run-smoke.ts` block the Discord notification path and the Windows operator path entirely:

1. **`require('node:https')` in an ESM module.** `package.json` declares `"type": "module"`, so `run-smoke.ts` runs as ESM and `require` is not defined. The call sits inside `notify()`'s `try/catch`, which silently swallows the `ReferenceError`. Net effect: every Discord notification (sprint start, success, failure) fails the first time `notify()` runs, so operators never see the webhook output even with `DISCORD_IFLEET_WEBHOOK` set.

2. **`symlinkSync(node_modules)` without a type argument.** On Windows without Developer Mode (or without elevation), Node defaults to a `'file'`/`'dir'` symlink and fails with `EPERM: operation not permitted`. The `setupWorktree` failure bubbles up to `markFailed`, so the smoke test cannot run on a fresh Windows box. On macOS/Linux the call works only by accident.

## What

- Replace `const { request } = require('node:https') as typeof import('node:https')` with a top-level `import { request } from 'node:https'`. ESM-correct, no runtime cost, and `request` is now obvious from the imports.
- In `setupWorktree`, select the symlink type by platform: `'junction'` on `win32` (no elevation required; NTFS junctions are sufficient because Node's `fs` resolves them as directories), `'dir'` elsewhere. Node ignores the type argument on POSIX, so this is a no-op on Mac/Linux.

Both changes are inside `scripts/run-smoke.ts` only. No public API change, no test fixture change.

## Tests

- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` — clean.
- `pnpm exec eslint scripts/run-smoke.ts` — clean.
- `pnpm exec eslint .` — only pre-existing `no-console` warning in `src/pipeline/runner.ts` (from #85), nothing new from this PR.
- `pnpm test` — 129/129 (vitest) + node:test runner clean.
- Husky pre-push — green.

No new tests added: `run-smoke.ts` is the smoke entry point itself and has no unit tests; the existing harness in `scripts/__tests__/smoke-email.test.ts` covers a different module (`smoke-email.ts`). The two fixes are mechanical platform/module-system corrections best validated by running the script (next sprint will exercise both paths in production).

## Out of scope

- Lote C (sprint persistence + auto-loop) still touches `src/orchestrator/sprint.ts` and `src/orchestrator/store.ts`. Not included here.
- Larger refactor of `notify()` (Discord retry, webhook URL validation) — out of scope; this PR only restores the existing behaviour under ESM.

## Operator checklist (pre-merge)

- [ ] #85 (Lote B) merged first to keep the diff minimal.
- [ ] After merging, re-run a smoke sprint on a fresh Windows machine without Developer Mode and confirm `setupWorktree` succeeds.
- [ ] Confirm Discord webhook delivers the `▶ Sprint started:` notification (proves I-6 fix landed end-to-end).

title: fix(workers): drop dead tool_result path; verify --include-partial-messages flag
body:
## Why

`src/workers/claude.ts` `parseClaudeEvent` had two branches that both emit `kind: 'tool_result'`. The first, triggered on `type === 'user'` messages, walked the nested message-content array looking for `tool_result`-shaped blocks and set `ok = !block.input`. That field is semantically meaningless on `tool_result` blocks — `input` belongs to `tool_use`, not `tool_result` — so every event the branch produced carried a fabricated `ok` flag.

Grepping the repo for consumers of `event.kind === 'tool_result'` (outside the adapters and their tests) finds zero call sites, so the bogus events were never read. The branch was both buggy and dead, but it sat in the middle of the parser ready to surface confusing behavior the first time a sprint started consuming `tool_result` events. Better to delete it than to leave a trap.

I-5 (was bundled in this batch): verify the `--include-partial-messages` flag still exists in the installed `claude` CLI. If it had been removed in a recent release, `buildClaudeArgs` would have produced an unknown-option error and the worker would have crashed silently via `WorkerCrashError`. On the runner used here (`claude --version` reports `2.1.142`), `claude --help` still lists the flag, so no change is needed in `buildClaudeArgs`. Documenting the check in the commit message and PR so the next reviewer does not re-run it.

## What

- Removed the `type === 'user'` arm from `parseClaudeEvent`. The remaining `type === 'tool_result'` arm is the authoritative path: it reads `is_error` per the Claude stream-json schema and yields `ok = is_error !== true`.
- Added a brief comment on the surviving `tool_result` arm explaining the deletion so the next person who sees the gap in the parser knows what used to be there and why.
- Dropped `tool_use_id?: string` from `ClaudeStreamEvent` — the field was declared but only ever accessed by the removed branch.
- Kept `--include-partial-messages` in `buildClaudeArgs` after verifying the installed CLI still supports it.

## Tests

Two new cases in `src/workers/__tests__/claude.test.ts`:

- `tool_result events read is_error from top-level event` — feeds two top-level `tool_result` events (one with `is_error: true`, one without) and asserts the emitted `ok` flag matches.
- `user messages with embedded tool_result blocks are ignored (no spurious events)` — feeds a `type === 'user'` message whose content nests a `tool_result`-shaped block, and asserts the adapter does **not** emit any `tool_result` events for it. This is the regression pin for the deleted branch.

The existing line-102 assertion in `spawn args include flags and session-id for new run` keeps pinning `--include-partial-messages` in `buildClaudeArgs`, so I-5 already has coverage and no new test is added for it.

Local verification: `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean. `pnpm exec eslint .` clean (1 pre-existing `no-console` warning in `pipeline/runner.ts:161`, untouched). `pnpm test` reports `127/127` vitest green and the husky pre-push hook passed. `claude.test.ts` was also run directly via `node --import tsx --test src/workers/__tests__/claude.test.ts` → 9/9 pass, confirming the two new cases land.

## Incidental

Same byte-identical cherry-pick of the Windows-only `screenshot.test.ts` fix that #84 and #85 carry. Without it the husky pre-push hook blocks this PR on Windows. No-op on rebase once one of those lands.

## Out of scope (other batches in the stabilization sprint)

- C-1 author allowlist (#84)
- C-2 budget cost propagation (#85)
- C-3 `sprintSpend` / `rateLimitResetAt` persistence — depends on #85
- I-4 `tick()` auto-loop in `orchestrator/sprint.ts`
- I-6 `require('node:https')` in ESM `scripts/run-smoke.ts`
- I-8 Windows `symlinkSync(node_modules)` fallback

## Operator checklist before merge

- [ ] Confirm the `claude` CLI version pinned in CI / `docs/running.md` still exposes `--include-partial-messages`. If a future bump removes it, `buildClaudeArgs` will need the flag dropped — the existing flag-presence test on line 102 of `claude.test.ts` will fail loudly when that happens.
- [ ] If #84 / #85 are already merged, the incidental `screenshot.test.ts` commit will be a no-op on rebase — squash if preferred.

title: fix(pipeline): propagate totalCostUsd so BUDGET_USD guard works end-to-end
body:
## Why

The Architect → Editor → Reviewer pipeline writes a per-attempt JSONL cost record to `.omc/costs.json` at the end of every PR. That file is the only durable signal the `BUDGET_USD` guard has to enforce the daily cap (and what `SprintManager.sprintSpend` needs to sum on boot once C-3 persists it).

Today the field that actually matters — `totalCostUsd` — is hard-coded to `0`. Claude's stream-json `result` event reports `total_cost_usd`, `workers/claude.ts` captures it into `WorkerResult.totalCostUsd`, and then the pipeline `SpawnResult` boundary drops it on the floor before it reaches `AttemptRecord`. Net: `BUDGET_USD=5.00` in `.env` does not enforce, because every recorded spend reads `0.00`.

The TODO in `runner.ts:logCosts` listed the exact 3-step wiring that was missing. This PR does it.

## What

- `SpawnResult` and `AttemptRecord` gain an optional `totalCostUsd?: number` (`src/pipeline/types.ts`). Spreads are used at every write site so `exactOptionalPropertyTypes` is honored — undefined stays undefined, not "set to undefined".
- `architect.ts`, `editor.ts`, `doctor.ts`, `reviewer.ts` (both full-reviewer and Haiku-gate paths) copy `result.totalCostUsd` onto the `AttemptRecord` they produce.
- `scripts/run-smoke.ts` `buildWorkerPool` forwards `r.totalCostUsd` from the underlying `WorkerResult` into the pipeline `SpawnResult`.
- `runner.ts:logCosts` now reads `attempt.totalCostUsd ?? 0`. The fallback to `0` keeps the JSONL well-formed for adapters that don't surface cost (Codex today). The obsolete TODO is removed and a brief comment explains why `0` is still possible.

## Tests

- `__tests__/helpers.ts` `ScriptedSpawn` gains an optional `totalCostUsd`, plus a `repoRoot` knob on `buildPipelineInput` so tests can point `logCosts` at a `mkdtemp` dir and inspect the actual file written.
- Two new cases in `runner.test.ts`:
  - **Adapter reports cost** → assert the value lands on each `AttemptRecord` *and* on the matching record in `.omc/costs.json`. Sum across roles checked.
  - **Adapter omits cost** → assert `attempt.totalCostUsd` stays `undefined` and the JSONL record stores `0` (so summarize/budget math doesn't NaN).

All `129/129` vitest tests pass locally. `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` clean. `pnpm exec eslint .` clean (one pre-existing `no-console` warning in `runner.ts:161`, untouched). Husky pre-push ran on push and passed.

## Incidental

This branch is based on `upstream/main`, which still has the Windows-only failure in `src/verify/__tests__/screenshot.test.ts` that #84 also includes. Cherry-picked the same 1-file test-side fix here so husky pre-push doesn't block this PR — the commit is byte-identical to #84's, so once #84 lands this becomes a trivial no-op on rebase.

## Out of scope (other batches in the stabilization sprint)

- C-1 author allowlist (#84)
- C-3 `sprintSpend` / `rateLimitResetAt` persistence across PM2 restarts (depends on this PR to sum real values)
- I-4 `tick()` auto-loop
- I-5 verify `--include-partial-messages` against installed `claude --help`
- I-6 `require('node:https')` in ESM `scripts/run-smoke.ts`
- I-7 `parseClaudeEvent` `type==='user'` tool_result heuristic
- I-8 Windows `symlinkSync(node_modules)` fallback

This PR does **not** start enforcing the budget on its own — `logCosts` already wrote records, and `SprintManager.sprintSpend` already sums them. Now those records carry real numbers, so the existing guard begins functioning end-to-end the first time a sprint runs after merge.

## Operator checklist before merge

- [ ] Spot-check `.omc/costs.json` after the next live sprint to confirm `totalCostUsd` is non-zero for Claude attempts and `0` for Codex attempts (expected, until a Codex cost source lands).
- [ ] If #84 is already merged, the incidental `screenshot.test.ts` commit will be a no-op on rebase — verify the diff is clean and squash if preferred.
- [ ] Decide whether to bump `BUDGET_USD` in `ecosystem.config.cjs` now that the cap will actually bite (current `5.00` is conservative for a single 4-role sprint on opus).

title: fix(queue): enforce author allowlist per repo to prevent issue-borne RCE
body:
## Why

The queue picks `auto:ship` issues from a public repo and feeds the body to a worker spawned as `claude -p --permission-mode auto`. Without an author filter, the path from `anyone-with-issue-write` → `arbitrary code on the runner` is two steps:

1. External user opens an issue with a malicious body (or prompt-injected body).
2. A CODEOWNER applies `auto:ship` (mass-triage, mis-click, social engineering).

`pickNext` then dispatches; the architect/editor read the body as their brief with auto-permission. RCE.

Branch-protection on `main` does not help — the worker runs *before* a PR exists.

## What

- `RepoRef.allowedAuthors?: ReadonlyArray<string>` on the queue type, surfaced as `RepoEntry.allowedAuthors` so it survives `loadReposConfig` + `migrateReposConfig`.
- `pickNext` and `watchForNew` skip issues whose `issue.user.login` is not in `repo.allowedAuthors`, with a `[queue] skipping …` warning.
- One-time constructor warning when a repo has no `allowedAuthors` set, so a misconfigured new repo is loud (not silent).
- Backwards compat: undefined or empty `allowedAuthors` ⇒ accept all authors (legacy mode). Means no breakage of existing test fixtures; the seeded config below makes the default posture strict.
- `config/repos.json` seeded with `"allowedAuthors": ["monstersebas1"]` for both configured repos.
- `QueuedTask.author: string` (non-optional) — `toTask` reads `issue.user?.login ?? ''`.

## Tests

- `isAuthorAllowed` pure function — accept / reject / legacy-undefined / legacy-empty-array.
- `pickNext` with allowlist — accept, reject, mixed-skip-then-accept, missing `user.login` rejected.
- `migrateReposConfig` preserves `allowedAuthors` and rejects non-string-array values.

All 129/129 vitest + node:test cases pass locally. Typecheck clean. ESLint clean (one pre-existing warning in `pipeline/runner.ts`, unrelated).

## Incidental

Second commit fixes a Windows-only failure in `src/verify/__tests__/screenshot.test.ts` — the assertion hard-coded forward-slash strings while `screenshotPaths` uses `path.resolve` (OS-native separator + Windows drive-root semantics). Source code unchanged; test-side fix only.

## Out of scope (next batches in the stabilization sprint)

- C-2 budget cost propagation (`pipeline/runner.ts logCosts` hardcoded `totalCostUsd: 0`)
- C-3 `sprintSpend` / `rateLimitResetAt` persistence across PM2 restarts
- I-4 `tick()` auto-loop (so workers do not idle 5 min between dispatches)
- I-5 verify `--include-partial-messages` against installed `claude --help`
- I-6 `require('node:https')` in ESM `scripts/run-smoke.ts`
- I-7 `parseClaudeEvent` `type==='user'` tool_result heuristic (`ok: !block.input`)
- I-8 Windows `symlinkSync(node_modules)` fallback

## Operator checklist before merge

- [ ] Confirm `monstersebas1` is the correct sole-author seed (vs adding the `Seb'sClaude` bot identity if it opens issues under a separate login).
- [ ] If the bot opens issues under a different login, add that login to `allowedAuthors` in the same PR or follow-up; without it, the bot's issues will be skipped silently.

title: fix(classifier,pipeline): sonnet floor for editor + empty-diff guard
body:
## Summary

Closes two reliability gaps where haiku silently produced empty diffs and the reviewer then burned tokens on \"no diff provided\":

- **Editor tier floor (classifier):** the editor was always one tier below the architect, so `architect=haiku → editor=haiku`. Haiku in `claude -p` print mode reliably returns `ok=true` while making zero file edits. Floor the editor tier at `sonnet` so the editor is never haiku.
- **Empty-diff guard (runner):** right after `runEditor`, bail with `failed('editor produced no diff — possible silent tool-use failure')` if `editor.diff` is whitespace-only, so the reviewer is never spawned with an empty diff.

## Before / after — editor tier by architect tier

| Architect | Editor (before) | Editor (after) |
|-----------|-----------------|----------------|
| haiku     | haiku           | **sonnet**     |
| sonnet    | haiku           | **sonnet**     |
| opus      | sonnet          | sonnet         |

## Test plan

- [x] `npm test` — 202 node tests + 115 vitest tests, all passing
- [x] `npm run typecheck` — clean
- [x] New classifier test: editor is floored at sonnet when architect is haiku
- [x] New classifier test: editor is sonnet when architect is sonnet (one-tier-below would be haiku, floor applies)
- [x] New runner test: editor returns ok with empty diff → `failed`, reviewer is never spawned
- [x] Pre-push hooks (typecheck + lint + test) green

title: fix(factory,orchestrator): wire adapter registry + repos config
body:
## Summary

Two pre-launch wiring fixes for previously orphaned modules.

### Fix A — Adapter registry into `buildWorkerPool` (BLOCKER)

`src/pipeline/factory.ts::buildWorkerPool` called `createClaudeAdapter()` directly, so the `IFLEET_ADAPTER` env var had no effect on the Architect → Editor → Reviewer pipeline. The orchestrator-level `WorkerAdapter` from `src/orchestrator/types.ts` exposes only `Promise<SpawnHandle>` with a single `done` promise — it drops the event stream, text output, `sessionId`, and per-spawn `workingDir`/`abortSignal`/`systemPrompt` that the pipeline depends on. Rather than widen that orchestrator contract, this PR introduces a parallel **pipeline-level** adapter registry over the lower-level `src/workers/types.ts WorkerAdapter`:

- new `src/workers/adapters/pipeline-registry.ts` — registry + `getActivePipelineAdapter()`
- barrel `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` re-exports it
- `src/pipeline/factory.ts` swaps `createClaudeAdapter()` → `getActivePipelineAdapter()`

Both registries read the same `IFLEET_ADAPTER` env var and share `DEFAULT_ADAPTER_NAME = 'claude-cli'`, so flipping the env var switches both paths consistently. Default `claude-cli` behavior is preserved.

### Fix B — `loadReposConfig()` into `Orchestrator` (HIGH)

`src/config/repos.ts` exported `loadReposConfig()` but nothing called it; `makeProductionFactory`'s `repoId` fell back to a hardcoded `'weautomatehq1/IFleet'` literal.

- `OrchestratorOptions` gains optional `reposConfig?: ReposMap`
- `Orchestrator` derives `this.repoId` from `repoId` → `reposConfig` first key → `DEFAULT_REPO_ID`, exposes it via `getRepoId()`
- new `startOrchestrator(opts)` helper loads `config/repos.json` and constructs an Orchestrator wired with the result — the seam any future production startup script should call

Tests with no `reposConfig` continue to use the `DEFAULT_REPO_ID` fallback unchanged.

## Files changed

- `src/orchestrator/index.ts` — `reposConfig` field, `repoId` resolution, `getRepoId()`, `startOrchestrator()` helper
- `src/pipeline/factory.ts` — swap to `getActivePipelineAdapter()`
- `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` — re-export pipeline-registry surface
- `src/workers/adapters/pipeline-registry.ts` *(new)* — pipeline-level adapter registry
- `src/orchestrator/__tests__/repos-config.test.ts` *(new)* — 4 tests covering `repoId` resolution + `startOrchestrator`
- `src/workers/adapters/__tests__/pipeline-registry.test.ts` *(new)* — 7 tests covering register/resolve/env-var routing

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `npm test` — 202 node:test + 111 vitest = **313 pass, 0 fail** (baseline 189+; +11 new tests)
- [x] `IFLEET_ADAPTER=claude-cli` (default) still resolves the existing `createClaudeAdapter()` factory
- [x] `loadReposConfig` is now reachable from `startOrchestrator()` — `getRepoId()` derives `'configured/repo'` from a fixture `repos.json` under test
- [ ] No production startup script constructs `Orchestrator` yet (only tests + `scripts/run-smoke.ts`, which uses `PipelineBridge` directly), so the new `startOrchestrator` seam will be exercised end-to-end once that entry point lands

## Notes for reviewer

The task brief stated `getActiveAdapter()` already satisfied the pipeline's contract because the registry wraps the same underlying adapter — that is true for the **inner** spawn, but the orchestrator-level `WorkerAdapter` wrapper deliberately narrows the surface (no events, no text output, no per-spawn workingDir/signal/systemPrompt). Substituting it directly into `buildWorkerPool` would have silently broken cancellation, role isolation, rate-limit counting, and the architect/reviewer prompts. The parallel pipeline registry is the smallest change that wires `IFLEET_ADAPTER` through without regressing those.

title: feat(workers): plug-in adapter registry + claude-cli backend
body:
## Summary

Ships **task-queue Phase 1 item 9**: a generic plug-in `WorkerAdapter` registry plus the initial `claude-cli` backend that wraps the existing Claude Code CLI spawn path.

The hard design goal — and the reason this was routed to Opus — is that adding any future backend (vLLM, Ollama, MLX, OpenAI/Anthropic API) must be a **one-file drop-in** with **zero changes** to the orchestrator. The registry's only contract is *"hand me back a `WorkerAdapter`"*, which keeps the seam compatible with every future backend below.

### Why plug-in

Per the launch task queue, IFleet needs to swap backends as the team layers in local 5090 inference, Anthropic API fallback, and per-vendor seats. Hardcoding a single spawn primitive in the orchestrator would force a refactor every time. The registry abstracts that out behind a name (`claude-cli`, `vllm-local`, …) selected by `IFLEET_ADAPTER` at runtime.

### Future-backend checklist

The contract handles each without schema changes — each reduces to "build a `WorkerAdapter` from config":

- [x] **`vllm-local`** — HTTP client → `http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions`. No new types.
- [x] **`ollama`** — HTTP client → `http://localhost:11434/v1`. No new types.
- [x] **`mlx`** — HTTP client → Apple Silicon MLX server. No new types.
- [x] **`anthropic-api`** — `@anthropic-ai/sdk` driven by `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. No new types.

Each future adapter ships as `src/workers/adapters/<name>.ts` + a single-line addition to `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` so the self-registration runs on import. The orchestrator stays untouched.

### Files

| File | Role |
| --- | --- |
| `src/workers/adapters/registry.ts` | name-keyed registry, `IFLEET_ADAPTER` env-var selection, JSDoc-documented future-backend contract |
| `src/workers/adapters/claude-cli.ts` | wraps `createClaudeAdapter`, reads `claude-max-1` from `config/workers.json`, maps `WorkerResult` → `SpawnResult` |
| `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` | barrel that imports every adapter file so self-registration runs |
| `src/workers/adapters/__tests__/registry.test.ts` | 11 tests: registration, resolution, unknown-name error, env-var selection, multi-adapter coexistence, last-wins overwrite |
| `src/workers/adapters/__tests__/claude-cli.test.ts` | 9 tests: contract satisfaction, inner-spawn delegation, model + profile resolution, cancel propagation, self-registration |

### Hard rules respected

- Pins to `claude-max-1` (single Max seat per Seb's no-rotation rule)
- Does not modify `src/orchestrator/types.ts`, `sprint.ts`, `pipeline-bridge.ts`, `workers.ts`, `pressure.ts`, `index.ts`
- Does not touch `src/workers/claude.ts`, `account-pool.ts`, or `config/workers.json`
- Beast Mode coding standard (named exports, no `any` without justification, etc.)

### Note for T3 / `PipelineRunnerFactory`

The orchestrator-level `WorkerAdapter` shape (`spawn(taskId, brief, opts) → Promise<SpawnHandle>`) is the contract the registry exposes. T3's `PipelineRunnerFactory` can read `getActiveAdapter()` from `src/workers/adapters/index.ts` at construction time — no other plumbing required. `IFLEET_ADAPTER` is unset by default, so v1 transparently routes to `claude-cli`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` — clean
- [x] `npm run lint` (eslint over `src/workers/adapters/`) — clean
- [x] `npm test` — 156 node:test + 72 vitest tests, all green (20 new tests in this PR)
- [x] Cancel propagation verified via `hangUntilSignal` fake-spawn fixture

title: feat(workers): launch hardening
body:
## Scope
Workers subsystem hardening for launch (`src/workers/**`). Owned by T3 of
session `20260513-2030-ifleet-launch`. Does NOT touch orchestrator, pipeline,
queue, observability, verify, or scripts.

## Files touched
- `src/workers/account-pool.ts` — extended (backwards-compatible).
- `src/workers/__tests__/account-pool.test.ts` — 7 new tests.
- `src/workers/__tests__/spawn-runner.test.ts` — 3 new tests.
- `src/workers/__tests__/claude.test.ts` — 1 new test.
- `src/workers/__tests__/codex.test.ts` — 2 new tests.
- `src/workers/__tests__/fake-spawn.ts` — adds `stdoutChunks` so tests can
  split a JSON line across multiple `data` events.

## What shipped — launch-readiness gaps closed
1. **Rotation around a rate-limited account.** `markRateLimited(id, retryAfterMs)`
   pauses a worker; `nextWorker()` skips paused workers transparently.
2. **All-accounts-paused doesn't hang.** New `AllAccountsPausedError` carries
   `nextAvailableMs` so the orchestrator can sleep until soonest recovery
   instead of spin-looping.
3. **Auth-failed accounts stay out for the session.** `markAuthFailed(id)`
   permanently disables a worker so a dead OAuth token can't keep coming back.
4. **Side-effect-free `acquire()`** returns `{ ok | all_paused | empty }` for
   callers that need to branch without throwing.
5. **Stream-json incremental parsing proven.** Tests feed each adapter a
   JSON object delivered as 4–6 chunks (only the last with `\n`) and assert
   the line is reassembled correctly. Guards against a future regression
   where someone replaces the line reader with a per-chunk `JSON.parse`.
6. **Stderr drainage proven.** Pumps 5,000 stderr lines through `runStreaming`
   and asserts the child still closes — i.e. the pipe is being drained as
   data arrives, not back-pressuring the child to a halt.
7. **Forced-cancel cleanup proven.** Cancels mid-stream while events are
   queued and asserts the result rejects with `WorkerCrashError`.
8. **Codex 401 is a hard error, not a retry.** A 401 from `codex exec --json`
   must NOT become a `rate_limit` event — otherwise the pool would rotate to
   the same dead credential forever. Test asserts the wire-level event is
   `error` with category `authentication_failed`.

## Test evidence (local)
- `npm run typecheck` — clean
- `npm run lint` — clean
- `npm test` — 140/140 node tests + 72/72 vitest tests pass

## What Seb should verify by hand
- Read `src/workers/account-pool.ts`. The new API is additive — `nextWorker()`
  still works for existing callers, the new methods are opt-in.
- Confirm the orchestrator PR (T1) can call `markRateLimited` /
  `markAuthFailed` from the `WorkerEvent` event stream without crossing
  this PR's boundary. The wire to consume the new API lands in T1.

## Out of scope (intentional)
- Orchestrator wiring of the new pool API (T1).
- Persisting pause state across orchestrator restarts (Phase B work).
- Per-worker concurrency tracking — the pool is still stateless about
  in-flight calls; callers layer that on top.

  - plan comment ends with `React with ✅` and does NOT instruct text replies (this string is the load-bearing contract for the reaction-polling logic in `src/queue/issue-commenter.ts`)

- **Doctor retry cap** — `DOCTOR_MAX_ATTEMPTS=2` enforced at `runner.ts` doctor cycle; covered by existing `runner.test.ts` "doctor retry limit" test
- **Cross-provider review** — `assertCrossProviderRule` runs both pre-flight in `runner.ts` and inside `reviewer.ts` as defense-in-depth; covered by `reviewer.test.ts` (both directions) and `runner.test.ts` (codex/codex e2e, now plus claude/claude e2e)
- **PR open gating** — guarded at the lastVerify check before PR open *and* the post-fix-pass re-verify gate; new test covers the latter explicitly
- **Prompt determinism** — `prompts.ts` is pure constants; other `${}` uses are over required typed fields. Only meaningful empty-fallback risk was empty plan, now guarded

## Out of scope

- `npm run lint` — clean
- `npm test` — 128 node tests + 79 vitest tests, 0 failures

## Verify by hand
- Confirm CI green on this PR.
- The claude/claude same-provider e2e test (`runner.test.ts`) and the verify-fails-after-fix-pass test are the new regression guards — these are the launch-blocking paths.

---
🗣️ **In plain terms:**
The pipeline already enforced the launch-critical rules (doctor only retries twice, the reviewer must use the opposite AI to the editor, no PR is opened if tests fail, the architect waits for your ✅ reaction on its plan comment). This PR doesn't change behavior — it adds tests that fail loudly if anyone breaks those rules later, plus one small safety check: if the architect ever returns a blank plan, the pipeline now bails cleanly instead of handing nothing to the editor. The patch about routing the architect to Opus only on `complexity:high` is outside this PR's scope and is parked for a follow-up because it touches the classifier and the smoke runner.

title: feat(classifier): reviewer >= architect invariant + complexity:low rule-cap test
body:
## Context
PR #41 introduced the policy that the architect role runs on Sonnet by default and is only promoted to Opus when the issue carries a `complexity:high` label. `complexity:low` is also supposed to force Sonnet even when something else (scorer or routing rule) would otherwise push to Opus.

The PR ships an explicit test for `complexity:low` defeating a **scorer-driven** opus promotion. It does not test the same outcome for a **rule-driven** opus promotion (the routing rules that hardcode opus for architect, like the SQL/migrations rule). The behavior is correct by code trace but not exercised by the test suite.

## Acceptance criteria
- A new unit test exists that:
  - Submits a task whose title or body matches a routing rule that maps `architect → claude-opus-4-7`
  - Includes `complexity:low` in the labels
  - Asserts the resulting architect model resolves to `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- A second unit test asserts the same outcome when no complexity label is present at all (the default cap), distinct from the `complexity:low` case
- All existing tests still pass

## Out of scope
- Changing the routing rule shape or schema
- Refactoring the classifier
- Adding new label types

## Notes
Independent reviewer of PR #41 flagged this as the single test gap worth a follow-up. Not a blocker for the merge.

**AFK** — agent can execute autonomously.

title: feat(classifier): complexity:high label gates architect opus
body:
## Summary
- Architect runs on **sonnet-4.6** by default. Only `complexity:high` on the issue promotes to **opus-4.7**.
- Scorer keywords (stripe/payment/auth/migration/etc.) and `routing.json` rules can no longer push the architect past sonnet on their own — explicit operator opt-in only.
- `complexity:low` semantics preserved by the second `architectModel === TIERS.opus` cap.
- Editor stays on claude; `routing.json` keeps a `switchable` field for codex for when Codex Pro is purchased.
- `scripts/run-smoke.ts` `mapModel` becomes a pass-through — classifier is now the single source of truth for model selection (obsoletes the Phase A cap).

## Why
Opus-4.7 hits the 5-hour Claude Max rate limit on longer briefs, surfacing as silent `code=1` stalls in Phase A. Making opus an explicit label-gated opt-in keeps the fleet shipping by default and reserves opus budget for briefs that actually need it.

## Test plan
- [x] `pnpm test` — 102 node:test + 72 vitest passing locally
- [x] Typecheck clean
- [x] New tests cover: `complexity:high` promotion, `complexity:low` block, scorer-keyword cap, rule-override cap, priority:high cap, editor tier still one below architect

## Notes
- Co-developed across two terminals on 2026-05-13: this session designed the policy after Seb locked the hybrid + explicit-label decision; a parallel audit session merged PR #40 (audit follow-ups) and resolved the classifier verify-union / opus-cap conflict before staging the patch.
- The `complexity === 'low' && architectTier === 'opus'` branch was removed during conflict resolution because the auto-cap above makes the `=== 'opus'` comparison unreachable (TS2367). The intent is preserved by the architectModel-level cap further down.

---
🗣️ In plain terms:
The fleet's "architect" agent used to silently jump to the most powerful model (Opus) whenever it saw words like "stripe" or "auth" — and Opus has a strict rate limit, so the whole fleet would stall. From now on, the architect uses the cheaper Sonnet model by default; Opus only kicks in if you put a `complexity:high` label on the issue. No more invisible stalls, no surprise spend. Codex is wired so it can be turned on later without a code change.

title: fix: audit follow-ups for PR #34 — orchestrator/queue/classifier/smoke hardening
body:
## Summary

Follow-ups from the post-PR-34 audit. Four critical, five high, one chore — grouped into five commits by concern. All 102 Node + 72 Vitest tests pass; `tsc --noEmit` clean.

## Commits

1. **`fix(orchestrator)`** — plug worker leak when `briefLoader.loadBrief()` throws (worker was acquired before the load and never released); harden `JSON.parse` on `state_json` / `required_capabilities_json` in `loadSprint`/`loadTask`/`listSprintsByStateKind`; `saveSprint.upsertTask` now persists `required_capabilities_json` so re-saves don't drop the column.
2. **`fix(queue)`** — `control-plane.ts` sends 202 before `dispatch()` and detaches dispatch with explicit `.catch()` (eliminates the silent failure when dispatch threw after headers were sent); `capability-bridge` replaces `void handleBlocked(...)` with `.catch(console.error)`; drop the unchecked `as string[]` cast on `event.payload.missing` for `Array.isArray` + type validation.
3. **`fix(classifier)`** — `classifyTask` now unions the matched rule's `verify[]` with `hints.verify` instead of dropping it. UI tasks matching `.tsx`/`components/` were silently losing the `playwright` verify the rule specified. New test covers this. `routing.json` rule 2 (refactor/rename/boilerplate) re-routed from `provider:codex` (no enabled worker — `codex-pro-1` is in `workers.json _pending`) to `provider:claude` so the rule actually fires.
4. **`fix(smoke,obs,docs)`** — capture the worker event loop in a const and `await` it inside `result()` so late worker errors aren't lost; wrap `titleToBranchName` + `setupWorktree` in try/catch with `teardownWorktree` on failure; `createBufferedFormatter` `emit()` is now try/caught so a Discord webhook failure doesn't silently break buffering; `tail-cli.ts` `COLORS` literals converted from raw ESC bytes to explicit `'\x1b[...m'`; `ARCHITECTURE.md` Architect line updated to mention the Phase A Sonnet cap applied by `mapModel`.
5. **`chore(pipeline)`** — `appendCostRecord` was being called with `totalCostUsd: 0` hardcoded. Claude already extracts `total_cost_usd` from its CLI stream, but the pipeline's `SpawnResult` / `AttemptRecord` drop the field at the worker→pipeline boundary. Wiring real cost is a multi-file change crossing `src/workers/types.ts` and the per-role adapters — out of scope for this PR. Replace the bare `0` with a TODO comment documenting the boundary. Honest 0 beats fake telemetry.

## Audit items addressed

| ID | Severity | Where |
|---|---|---|
| C1 | Critical | `orchestrator/sprint.ts` worker leak on brief-load throw |
| C2 | Critical | `queue/control-plane.ts` response race |
| C3 | Critical | `classifier/index.ts` rule `verify[]` field discarded |
| C4 | Critical | `scripts/run-smoke.ts` fire-and-forget event loop |
| H1 | High | `queue/capability-bridge.ts` `void handleBlocked` swallows errors |
| H2 | High | `queue/capability-bridge.ts` unchecked `as string[]` |
| H3 | High | `orchestrator/store.ts` unprotected `JSON.parse` |
| H4 | High | `orchestrator/store.ts` `saveSprint` missing column |
| H5 | High | `observability/discord.ts` `emit()` not error-protected |
| H6 | High | `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` Architect/Opus doc drift |
| H7 | High | `scripts/run-smoke.ts` worktree leak on setup throw |
| H8 | High | `config/routing.json` codex rule with no enabled worker |
| H9 | High | `pipeline/runner.ts` hardcoded `totalCostUsd: 0` |
| L4 | Low | `observability/tail-cli.ts` ANSI codes |

## Not in this PR (deferred)

- **Real cost wiring** — needs `SpawnResult` / `AttemptRecord` boundary fixed across `src/workers/types.ts`, `src/pipeline/types.ts`, and the four per-role adapters (`architect.ts`/`editor.ts`/`reviewer.ts`/`doctor.ts`). Track as a follow-up.
- **Dead exports** (M2 `parseRequiredCapabilities`, M4 `readCostLog` / `summarizeCosts`, L3 `@octokit/plugin-throttling`, husky) — left for a separate cleanup pass once it's clear which are about to be wired.
- **Test glob unification** (M9) — `npm test` runs Node `--test` on orchestrator/queue/workers and Vitest on the rest, but the classifier glob is in neither runner. Worth a dedicated PR.
- **Capability-blocked state machine consistency** (M3) — `tick` writes `state='failed'` directly, bypassing `VALID_SPRINT_TRANSITIONS`. Needs a design call on whether to add a `capability_blocked` task state.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `pnpm test` — 102 Node tests + 72 Vitest tests, all green
- [ ] Smoke driver end-to-end against a real issue (Seb to run after merge)

title: fix(scripts): derive branch name from issue title in run-smoke.ts

## Problem

The branch name in `run-smoke.ts` is hardcoded:
```ts
const branchName = `chore/smoke-${rawTask.issueNumber}-remove-stale-todo`;
```

This only works for issue #6. Any new issue will get a branch named after the stale TODO task.

## Solution

Derive the branch name from `rawTask.title` at runtime:
- Lowercase the title
- Replace spaces and special chars with `-`
- Truncate to ~50 chars
- Prefix with `chore/smoke-<issueNumber>-`

Example: issue #7 "feat: add classifier module" → `feat/smoke-7-add-classifier-module`

## Acceptance criteria

- Branch name reflects the picked issue's title
- No hardcoded string references
- Typecheck + lint + test pass

title: fix(workers): remove --bare flag that breaks OAuth/keychain auth
body:
## Summary
- Removes `--bare` from `buildClaudeArgs` in the Claude worker adapter.

## Root cause
`--bare` sets `CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE=1` and explicitly skips all keychain reads. Claude Max accounts authenticate via OAuth stored in the keychain — no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. With `--bare`, the worker process exits with code 1 immediately on auth failure.

## Fix
Remove `--bare`. Workers now use the standard auth path (keychain/OAuth). The test that checked `--bare` is present now checks that it is absent.

## Phase B note
`--bare` was originally intended to isolate workers from user session hooks and CLAUDE.md. That isolation goal remains valid but needs a different mechanism (e.g. `--settings` pointing at a minimal fleet config). Tracked as a Phase B item.

## Test plan
- [ ] 128 tests pass (CI will confirm)
- [ ] Smoke re-run: `npx tsx scripts/run-smoke.ts` no longer exits with code 1 on architect spawn

title: feat(orchestrator): core sprint runtime, state store, pressure tracker [T1]
body:
## Technical
- Built `src/orchestrator/` with: `types.ts`, `store.ts`, `pressure.ts`, `workers.ts`, `sprint.ts`, `index.ts`, plus `__tests__/`.
- Exposed public types: `SprintId`, `TaskId`, `WorkerId`, `SprintMode`, `SprintState`, `SprintRecord`, `OrchestratorEvent`, `RateLimitSnapshot`, plus support shapes (`TaskState`, `TaskRecord`, `SpawnHandle`, `SpawnResult`) and placeholder interfaces `WorkerAdapter` (TODO: T2) and `TaskBriefLoader` (TODO: T3).
- SQLite via `better-sqlite3` at `~/.omc/ifleet/state.db`. Five tables (`sprints`, `tasks`, `attempts`, `events`, `rate_limits`) with idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` migrations on construct. WAL + foreign keys enabled.
- Sprint state machine in `sprint.ts` with explicit transition table (`VALID_SPRINT_TRANSITIONS`). `startSprint`, `tick`, `cancelSprint`, plus `resumeAbandoned()` which resets running/assigned tasks back to `pending` after a crash.
- Kill switch: `Orchestrator` polls `<killFlagDir>/<sprintId>/cancel.flag` every 5s (configurable). On detection it calls `cancelSprint`.
- Rate-limit pressure tracker reads `{ tokensRemaining, tokensLimit, resetAt }`. Pressure clamped to [0, 1]; dispatch blocked at `>= 0.85`; `nextAvailableSlot` returns `resetAt` while blocked, falls to `now` after reset.
- Worker registry reads `config/workers.json` (treated as read-only), hot-reloads via `fs.watch` with a 100ms debounce, tracks in-flight count per worker against `maxConcurrent`.
- Tests use `node:test` via `tsx` (no Jest/Vitest dep). 28 cases covering transition table validity, dispatch + completion, failure path, pressure gating, cancel + idempotency, crash recovery, idempotent migrations, and a full kill-switch integration through the public `Orchestrator` API. All green.
- `package.json`: added `better-sqlite3`, `nanoid`, `@types/better-sqlite3`. `package-lock.json` intentionally not committed (per MASTER rules).

## Plain English
This is the brain of the fleet. It picks tasks off the queue, hands them to workers, keeps track of which tasks are running where, and notices when a worker is running out of rate-limit budget. If the process crashes, it remembers where it was and picks up. If Seb hits `/cancel`, it stops cleanly without losing work.

## Placeholder imports (for the reviewer)
- `WorkerAdapter` in `types.ts` — expects `spawn(taskId, brief, opts) -> { workerId, taskId, cancel(), done: Promise<SpawnResult> }`. T2 will provide.
- `TaskBriefLoader` in `sprint.ts` — expects `loadBrief(taskId) -> Promise<string>`. T3 will provide.

## Files touched (allowlist only)
- `src/orchestrator/**` (new)
- `package.json` (added deps)
