title: feat(pipeline): Architect → Editor → Reviewer pipeline (T4)
body:
## Technical
- Built `src/pipeline/`: `architect.ts`, `editor.ts`, `reviewer.ts`, `doctor.ts`, `pr.ts`, `runner.ts`, plus shared `types.ts` and `prompts.ts`.
- Three-role pipeline: **Architect plans → Editor codes → Reviewer checks the diff cold**.
- **Cross-provider reviewer rule** enforced (`assertCrossProviderRule`) — reviewer.provider MUST be the opposite of editor.provider, asserted at runtime and unit-tested both ways.
- **Doctor** handles CI failure: receives full untruncated CI log + brief + plan + diff, returns `{ rootCause, proposedFix, confidence, requiresNewBrief }`. Capped at 2 invocations per task; on `requiresNewBrief: true` it escalates immediately.
- **Reviewer retry loop**: on `request_changes` with attempts < 2 the editor runs a fix pass; on attempts >= 2 the pipeline returns `blocked_by_reviewer`.
- **`autonomy: 'review'`**: architect posts the plan to the issue and waits on `IssueCommenter.waitForApproval` (poll 30 s, timeout 8 h) for ✅ from `@monstersebas1` before the editor runs. `autonomy: 'auto'` proceeds immediately.
- **Editor safety**: system prompt forbids touching `main`, forbids `--no-verify`, forbids committing `.env` / `*.pem` / `*.key`.
- **Reviewer freshness**: spawned without `resumeSessionId` so the pool issues a new session — guaranteed not to reuse the editor's context.
- **AbortSignal** checked between every phase; mid-pipeline cancel returns `status: 'cancelled'`.
- **Tests** (23 passing): happy path, reviewer reject → retry → approve, reviewer keeps rejecting → blocked, doctor retry limit, cross-provider rule (both violations + both valid pairs), autonomy:review (approved + denied), cancel before-architect + mid-pipeline.
- Placeholder shapes for `WorkerPool` (T2) and `VerifyRunner` (T5) declared in `types.ts` so the pipeline compiles and tests independently; integration points are the only contract we need from those owners.

## Plain English

This is the three-step pipeline that runs for every task. One model writes the plan, a different model writes the code, and a THIRD model (from the other provider) reviews the diff. The fleet never lets the same model approve its own work. If CI breaks, a doctor agent reads the full log and proposes a fix — it gets two tries before giving up. If the reviewer keeps blocking, the PR is marked `blocked_by_reviewer` and a human has to step in. For tasks marked `autonomy: review`, the architect posts the plan to the GitHub issue and waits up to 8 hours for a ✅ reaction before the editor touches code.

title: feat(workers): claude + codex spawn adapters
body:
## Technical
- Built Claude adapter (`src/workers/claude.ts`): spawn `claude -p ... --output-format stream-json --include-partial-messages --bare` and parse `system/init`, `system/api_retry`, `assistant` (text + tool_use), `user` (tool_result), `result` (final text + `total_cost_usd`).
- Built Codex adapter (`src/workers/codex.ts`): spawn `codex exec --json --sandbox workspace-write --output-last-message <tmp>` and parse `thread.started`, `turn.*`, `item.*` (assistant_message / tool_call), `error`.
- Shared types (`src/workers/types.ts`): `WorkerAdapter`, `SpawnOpts`, `SpawnHandle`, `WorkerEvent`, `WorkerResult`, `WorkerCrashError`, `RateLimitCategory` + `categorizeRateLimitError` mapping HTTP status to one of the 8 categories.
- Pure streaming runner (`src/workers/spawn-runner.ts`): newline-delimited line reader, bounded stderr tail (last 100 lines by default), AbortSignal wiring, SIGTERM → 5s grace → SIGKILL cancellation. Accepts a `spawnImpl` for test injection so real binaries never run in tests.
- Resume: `--resume <sessionId>` (Claude) / `exec resume <id>` (Codex) when `sessionId` is provided in `SpawnOpts`.
- Crash safety: non-zero exit rejects the `result` promise with `WorkerCrashError { exitCode, signal, stderrTail }`.
- 15 tests via `node --test` covering parsing, rate-limit categorization, args construction, resume, cancel-with-SIGKILL-escalation, AbortSignal, stderr-tail bounding, and non-zero exit handling. All pass; `npm run typecheck` clean.

### Files touched outside the worker allowlist
- `tsconfig.json` — one line: `"allowImportingTsExtensions": true`. Required because Node 24's strip-only TypeScript mode resolves sibling `.ts` files only with explicit `.ts` extensions, and TS rejects those extensions by default. The task's own commands (`npm run typecheck` + `node --test src/workers/__tests__/*.ts`) only coexist with this flag. Calling it out for T4's review.
- `package-lock.json` was not committed (denylist).
- `src/.gitkeep` deleted (now redundant — real files exist).

## Plain English
This is what actually runs Claude and Codex on your machine. Each adapter
opens the right command, watches the JSON it streams back line by line,
catches rate-limit warnings and turns them into clean events the rest of
the fleet can act on, and reports a final result when the model is done.
If a run gets stuck, calling `cancel()` politely asks it to stop and
then force-kills after 5 seconds. If a session was interrupted, passing
the previous `sessionId` resumes it. Everything else in IFleet that
needs a model to do work will go through these two files.
